issue_title: makeotf - 'lookup useExtension' does not work right with contextual substitution lookups.
issue_body: When a  contextual lookup is made into an extension lookup, the actual contextual rules do go in extension subtables. However, the substitution rules that are applied by the contextual rules are accumulated in a separate lookup, whose subtables are NOT made into extension subtables. The problem code has been identified, but it will be a few days before the fix makes into a release.  This bug was found by Edgar Walthert, who is the first to push on this boundary.

issue_title: Unnecessary truncation of the Format 4 'cmap' subtable
issue_body:
When building CID-keyed fonts with a large number of mappings that result in a large number of segments in the Format 4 'cmap' subtable, _makeotf_ seems to give up too easily and issues the following warning:

`makeotfexe [WARNING] <Format4Test-Regular> cmap{plat=3,script=1,lang=0}: cmap format 4 subtable was truncated to 2 segments, due to overflow with non-truncated version`

However, if the resulting 'cmap' table is dumped using _ttx_ and the mappings are manually added, a well-formed Format 4 'cmap' subtable seems to result. See the "commands.txt" file in the [Format4Test.zip](https://github.com/adobe-type-tools/afdko/files/1636667/Format4Test.zip) ZIP file for more details and the steps to reproduce and manually fix using a test font based on [Adobe-CNS1-7](https://github.com/adobe-type-tools/Adobe-CNS1/).

issue_title: Integrating NDB into gcloud.datastore?
issue_body:
Had a few discussions with @GoogleCloudPlatform/cloud-datastore (particularly @pcostell) so I wanted to summarize the things we covered. This issue is to clarify the goals for Datastore support in gcloud-python, and discuss and decide on a course of action that everyone likes for how to reconcile what we have today with the other libraries out there.

---

### Current state of the world

Currently, I see two styles that people want to use when interacting with Datastore:

1. a "simple" more key-value-based style (similar to Amazon's Simple DB) where you're CRUDding dictionaries and then adding some querying
1. a more advanced ORM style where you're create models, and have some sort of schema defined in a Python file.

For the former, `gcloud.datastore` has had the goal of covering this use case. For the latter, `ndb` is the latest way (supported) way of doing this -- with others potentially existing, but `ndb` seems to be the clear leader.

We also have a unique situation where our code currently might have trouble running in App Engine -- whereas `ndb` can't run *outside* of App Engine. The layout sort of looks like this:

![current state](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/112928/5779860/4e3edeec-9d74-11e4-88aa-06ea07d878f1.png)

### Looking forward

If our goals are ....

1. We agree that both styles of interacting with Datastore matter (and both should exist)
1. `gcloud.datastore` and `ndb` are our choices for each style respectively
1. `gcloud.datastore` and `ndb` should both run in App Engine *and* non-App Engine runtimes
1. `gcloud.datastore` is where all the recommended Python stuff to talk to Datastore should live (it is the "official source of truth")
1. People who want to write new Python libraries for Datastore can rely on code that exists in `gcloud.datastore` (and set `gcloud` as a Python dependency)

... then I'd like to suggest that we....

1. Port `ndb` over as `gcloud.datastore.ndb` (bringing with it `datastore_rpc` and `datastore_query`)
1. Rewrite `gcloud.datastore` to run on top of `datastore_query`
1. Rename `gcloud.datastore` to be a peer with `ndb` (using "simple" in this diagram, not set on that at all though).

which makes things look like this:

![future state](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/112928/5780010/39a9a664-9d75-11e4-8a31-60b44f24ed4b.png)

What do you guys think?

/cc @GoogleCloudPlatform/cloud-datastore @dhermes @tseaver @silvolu @proppy

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
...

I am using the following versions of the API:
```
google-cloud-datastore==1.7.0
google-cloud-logging==1.6.0

```

Running Python 2.7 on Google Cloud Flexible with the following app.yaml:
```
runtime: custom
api_version: 1
service: vm

env: flex
entrypoint: gunicorn -b :$PORT main:app
beta_settings:

It seems that requests to the datastore are timing out with 504 errors even after retrying many times. To better debug the issue, I monkey patched the `google.api_core.retry_target` function which inserts more logging on retry calls. The monkey patch is as follows ([compare to original funciton here](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/api_core/google/api_core/retry.py#L140)):

```
def improve_retry_logging():
    '''Improve debug logging for our retry handler.'''
    try:
        from google.api_core import retry
    except AllImportErrors as e:
        return
    logging.info("Monkey patching retry.retry_target: %s", retry)
    def new_retry_target(target, predicate, sleep_generator, deadline,
            on_error = None):
        if deadline is not None:
            deadline_datetime = (
                retry.datetime_helpers.utcnow() +
                retry.datetime.timedelta(seconds=deadline))
        else:
            deadline_datetime = None
        last_exc = None
        total_sleep = 0.0
        for sleep_i, sleep in enumerate(sleep_generator):
            try:
                result = target()
            # pylint: disable=broad-except
            # This function explicitly must deal with broad exceptions.
            except Exception as exc:
                if not predicate(exc):
                    raise
                last_exc = exc
                if on_error is not None:
                    on_error(exc)
            else:
                if sleep_i > 0:
                    logging.info('retry_target success. Retries: %d. '
                        'Slept %.2f Secs', sleep_i, total_sleep)
                return result

            now = retry.datetime_helpers.utcnow()
            if deadline_datetime is not None and deadline_datetime < now:
                logging.warning('retry_target deadline reached. Retries: %d.'
                    'Deadline: %.1f S. Slept: %.2f S, Last %.2f S. Err: %s',
                        sleep_i, deadline, total_sleep, last_exc)
                retry.six.raise_from(
                    retry.exceptions.RetryError('Retry ({}) deadline of '
                        '{:.1f}s exceeded while calling {}'.format(
                            sleep_i, deadline, target,), last_exc), last_exc)
            logging.info('retry_target retry due to "%s" %s (%d), sleep %.2fs',
                last_exc, target, sleep_i, sleep)
            retry.time.sleep(sleep)
            total_sleep += sleep
        raise ValueError('Sleep generator stopped yielding sleep values.')
    # Here we do the monkey patch.
    retry.retry_target = new_retry_target
improve_retry_logging()
````

With the logging instrumentation above, you can clearly see the retry handler being hit multiple times, and failing every single time with these 504 errors.

import google.cloud.datastore

    entity = google.cloud.datastore.Entity(key=client.key('spam'))
    client.put(entity)


# Fails
@freeze_time('3216-08-04')
def test_put_frozen():
    client = google.cloud.datastore.Client()
    entity = google.cloud.datastore.Entity(key=client.key('spam'))

The datastore was launched in the background using the following code:

```py
def start_datastore():
    """
    Start an in memory gcloud datastore emulator.

    The datastore and all of its subprocesses are killed when the Python
    interpreter is shut down.

    Raises:
        RuntimeError: If the datastore failed to start.

    """
    tmpdir = tempfile.TemporaryDirectory()
    atexit.register(partial(tmpdir.cleanup))
    command = (
        'gcloud',
        'beta',
        'emulators',
        'datastore',
        'start',
        '--project', os.environ['GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT'],
        '--no-store-on-disk',
        '--data-dir', tmpdir.name,
        '--consistency', '1.0'
    )
    proc = subprocess.Popen(
        command,
        stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
        preexec_fn=os.setsid)
    atexit.register(partial(os.killpg, os.getpgid(proc.pid), signal.SIGTERM))
    logs = []
    for lineno, line in enumerate(proc.stderr):
        line = line.decode()
        logs.append(line)
        if 'Dev App Server is now running' in line:
            return tmpdir
        # At the time of writing, 22 lines were printed before the
        # server was up and running. A small buffer is allowed in case
        # changes are introduced in the logging output.
        if lineno > 40:
            break
    raise RuntimeError('Unable to start datastore\n' + ''.join(logs))
```

issue_title: pip installation dependencies inconsistency
issue_body:
Hi,

When pip installing version 0.24.0, I think there is an inconsistency in the dependencies with google-cloud-error-reporting. In the 0.24.0 tagged release, which is what pip install google-cloud picks up, the setup.py associated with it has `google-cloud-error-reporting >=0.24.0,<0.25dev`. See https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/0.24.0/setup.py

Because google-cloud-error-reporting cut a new release of 0.24.1 (see https://pypi.python.org/pypi/google-cloud-error-reporting), which itself requires `google-cloud-logging > 1.0.0` (https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/error_reporting/setup.py#L55) it creates an inconsistency with the `google-cloud-logging > 0.24.0,<0.25dev` (https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/0.24.0/setup.py#L60). 

While `pip install google-cloud` functions correctly and you'll be able to import all the packages and use them until there is an api inconsistency, if you have a script that is installed that requires google-cloud, pkg_resources will fail.  This comes up in packages that I build that install a script through setup.py in the scripts=[] section. It installs the script and at the top puts something like:

```
__requires__ = 'my_package'
__import__('pkg_resources').require('my_package')
```
When this gets executed and my_package requires `google-cloud`, then the pkg_resources for google-cloud get checked.

To reproduce on python 2.7.6:
```
pip install google-cloud==0.24.0

python -c "__requires__=['google-cloud']; import pkg_resources"
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 3036, in <module>
    @_call_aside
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 3020, in _call_aside
    f(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 3049, in _initialize_master_working_set
    working_set = WorkingSet._build_master()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 656, in _build_master
    return cls._build_from_requirements(__requires__)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 669, in _build_from_requirements
    dists = ws.resolve(reqs, Environment())
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 859, in resolve
    raise VersionConflict(dist, req).with_context(dependent_req)
pkg_resources.ContextualVersionConflict: (google-cloud-logging 0.24.0 (/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages), Requirement.parse('google-cloud-logging<2.0dev,>=1.0.0'), set(['google-cloud-error-reporting']))

```

I realize this is a super hard thing to guard against with so many sub-packages and inter-dependencies, but maybe a test that just does the above __requires__ and import pkg_resources on previous pip-installed tagged versions would help? Maybe you have something like this already on `master`, since if you manually clone the google-cloud-python repository and run `setup.py develop` or `install`, the above pkg_resources error does not show up.

I think a fix might be removing the 0.24.1 google-cloud-error-reporting pypi release and re-releasing as 0.25.0, but I'm not sure that's a great option. Alternatively release a 0.24.1 google-cloud that pegs `google-cloud-error-reporting==0.24.0`?

issue_title: Add JSON Documentation
issue_body:
This issue is for tracking the requirements, issues and solutions in generating JSON for the [gcloud-common Angular site](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-common/blob/5a0d68a68a51df16e6fac3da2c84b02a3ae299e8/site/README.md).
## Known Requirements
- [ ] Extracting sphinx code examples and place them in `methods[{examples:[]}]`.
- [ ] Generate table of contents from sphinx toc. (index.rst).
- [ ] Class linking should be added. i.e. support :class:`gcloud.datastore._generated.query_pb2.Query`, this is partially done through [types.json](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-common/blob/5a0d68a68a51df16e6fac3da2c84b02a3ae299e8/site/README.md#types).
- [ ] Source code links (base on relative repo path. i.e. `/google/cloud/datastore/client.py#L25`).
- [ ] "Getting Started" and "Guide" type pages need to be ported.
- [ ] Ensure that snippets are pulled in properly from `docs/*_snippets.py`.
- [ ] Turn on auto `gcloud-common/site` [deployments](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-common/blob/5a0d68a68a51df16e6fac3da2c84b02a3ae299e8/deploy-docs.sh#L80).
## Discussions
- How much data is available in Writers https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/sphinx-users/-J1IOOBd-0g
## Resources
- http://www.arnebrodowski.de/blog/write-your-own-restructuredtext-writer.html
- https://sourceforge.net/p/docutils/code/HEAD/tree/trunk/docutils/
- https://github.com/sphinx-doc/sphinx
- https://bitbucket.org/birkenfeld/sphinx-contrib

issue_title: Cloud speech veneer should not provide a default sample rate
issue_body: Default sample rates should be removed.  For audio types with a header the server can automatically determine the sample rate. (We are implementing this for V1.)  For audio types without a header the default causes more harm than good because if it is incorrect the results could be empty or wildly inaccurate, which causes user confusion.

issue_title: enable byref=False for classes defined in modules
issue_body:
See: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/32363312/why-dill-dumps-external-classes-by-reference-no-matter-what

```
# file: foo.py
# 
class Foo:
    y = 1
    def bar( self, x ):
        return x + Foo.y
```
We also define `Foo` dynamically, for comparison.
```
>>> import dill
>>> import foo
>>> 
>>> class Foo:
...     y = 1
...     def bar( self, x ):
...         return x + Foo.y
... 
>>> f = Foo()
>>> ff = foo.Foo()
```
So when Foo is defined in `__main__`, `byref` is respected.
```
>>> dill.dumps(f, byref=False)              
b'\x80\x03cdill.dill\n_create_type\nq\x00(cdill.dill\n_load_type\nq\x01X\x04\x00\x00\x00typeq\x02\x85q\x03Rq\x04X\x03\x00\x00\x00Fooq\x05h\x01X\x06\x00\x00\x00objectq\x06\x85q\x07Rq\x08\x85q\t}q\n(X\r\x00\x00\x00__slotnames__q\x0b]q\x0cX\x03\x00\x00\x00barq\rcdill.dill\n_create_function\nq\x0e(cdill.dill\n_unmarshal\nq\x0fC]\xe3\x02\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x02\x00\x00\x00\x02\x00\x00\x00C\x00\x00\x00s\x0b\x00\x00\x00|\x01\x00t\x00\x00j\x01\x00\x17S)\x01N)\x02\xda\x03Foo\xda\x01y)\x02\xda\x04self\xda\x01x\xa9\x00r\x05\x00\x00\x00\xfa\x07<stdin>\xda\x03bar\x03\x00\x00\x00s\x02\x00\x00\x00\x00\x01q\x10\x85q\x11Rq\x12c__builtin__\n__main__\nh\rNN}q\x13tq\x14Rq\x15X\x07\x00\x00\x00__doc__q\x16NX\n\x00\x00\x00__module__q\x17X\x08\x00\x00\x00__main__q\x18X\x01\x00\x00\x00yq\x19K\x01utq\x1aRq\x1b)\x81q\x1c.'
>>> dill.dumps(f, byref=True)
b'\x80\x03c__main__\nFoo\nq\x00)\x81q\x01.'
>>>
```
However, when the class is defined in a module, `byref` is not respected.
```
>>> dill.dumps(ff, byref=False)
b'\x80\x03cfoo\nFoo\nq\x00)\x81q\x01.'
>>> dill.dumps(ff, byref=True)
b'\x80\x03cfoo\nFoo\nq\x00)\x81q\x01.'
```
Note, that I wouldn't use the recurse option in this case, as `Foo.y` will likely infinitely recurse. That's also something that I believe there's current ticket for, but if there isn't, there should be.

Let's dig a little deeper… what if we modify the instance…
```
>>> ff.zap = lambda x: x + ff.y
>>> _ff = dill.loads(dill.dumps(ff))
>>> _ff.zap(2)
3
>>> dill.dumps(ff, byref=True)
b'\x80\x03cfoo\nFoo\nq\x00)\x81q\x01}q\x02X\x03\x00\x00\x00zapq\x03cdill.dill\n_create_function\nq\x04(cdill.dill\n_unmarshal\nq\x05CY\xe3\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x01\x00\x00\x00\x02\x00\x00\x00C\x00\x00\x00s\x0b\x00\x00\x00|\x00\x00t\x00\x00j\x01\x00\x17S)\x01N)\x02\xda\x02ff\xda\x01y)\x01\xda\x01x\xa9\x00r\x04\x00\x00\x00\xfa\x07<stdin>\xda\x08<lambda>\x01\x00\x00\x00s\x00\x00\x00\x00q\x06\x85q\x07Rq\x08c__builtin__\n__main__\nX\x08\x00\x00\x00<lambda>q\tNN}q\ntq\x0bRq\x0csb.'
>>> dill.dumps(ff, byref=False)
b'\x80\x03cfoo\nFoo\nq\x00)\x81q\x01}q\x02X\x03\x00\x00\x00zapq\x03cdill.dill\n_create_function\nq\x04(cdill.dill\n_unmarshal\nq\x05CY\xe3\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x01\x00\x00\x00\x02\x00\x00\x00C\x00\x00\x00s\x0b\x00\x00\x00|\x00\x00t\x00\x00j\x01\x00\x17S)\x01N)\x02\xda\x02ff\xda\x01y)\x01\xda\x01x\xa9\x00r\x04\x00\x00\x00\xfa\x07<stdin>\xda\x08<lambda>\x01\x00\x00\x00s\x00\x00\x00\x00q\x06\x85q\x07Rq\x08c__builtin__\n__main__\nX\x08\x00\x00\x00<lambda>q\tNN}q\ntq\x0bRq\x0csb.'
>>> 
```
No biggie, it pulls in the dynamically added code. However, we'd probably like to modify `Foo` and not the instance.
```
>>> Foo.zap = lambda self,x: x + Foo.y
>>> dill.dumps(f, byref=True)
b'\x80\x03c__main__\nFoo\nq\x00)\x81q\x01.'
>>> dill.dumps(f, byref=False)
b'\x80\x03cdill.dill\n_create_type\nq\x00(cdill.dill\n_load_type\nq\x01X\x04\x00\x00\x00typeq\x02\x85q\x03Rq\x04X\x03\x00\x00\x00Fooq\x05h\x01X\x06\x00\x00\x00objectq\x06\x85q\x07Rq\x08\x85q\t}q\n(X\x03\x00\x00\x00barq\x0bcdill.dill\n_create_function\nq\x0c(cdill.dill\n_unmarshal\nq\rC]\xe3\x02\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x02\x00\x00\x00\x02\x00\x00\x00C\x00\x00\x00s\x0b\x00\x00\x00|\x01\x00t\x00\x00j\x01\x00\x17S)\x01N)\x02\xda\x03Foo\xda\x01y)\x02\xda\x04self\xda\x01x\xa9\x00r\x05\x00\x00\x00\xfa\x07<stdin>\xda\x03bar\x03\x00\x00\x00s\x02\x00\x00\x00\x00\x01q\x0e\x85q\x0fRq\x10c__builtin__\n__main__\nh\x0bNN}q\x11tq\x12Rq\x13X\x07\x00\x00\x00__doc__q\x14NX\r\x00\x00\x00__slotnames__q\x15]q\x16X\n\x00\x00\x00__module__q\x17X\x08\x00\x00\x00__main__q\x18X\x01\x00\x00\x00yq\x19K\x01X\x03\x00\x00\x00zapq\x1ah\x0c(h\rC`\xe3\x02\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x02\x00\x00\x00\x02\x00\x00\x00C\x00\x00\x00s\x0b\x00\x00\x00|\x01\x00t\x00\x00j\x01\x00\x17S)\x01N)\x02\xda\x03Foo\xda\x01y)\x02\xda\x04self\xda\x01x\xa9\x00r\x05\x00\x00\x00\xfa\x07<stdin>\xda\x08<lambda>\x01\x00\x00\x00s\x00\x00\x00\x00q\x1b\x85q\x1cRq\x1dc__builtin__\n__main__\nX\x08\x00\x00\x00<lambda>q\x1eNN}q\x1ftq Rq!utq"Rq#)\x81q$.'
```
Ok, that's fine, but what about the `Foo` in our external module?
```
>>> ff = foo.Foo()
>>> 
>>> foo.Foo.zap = lambda self,x: x + foo.Foo.y
>>> dill.dumps(ff, byref=False)
b'\x80\x03cfoo\nFoo\nq\x00)\x81q\x01.'
>>> dill.dumps(ff, byref=True)
b'\x80\x03cfoo\nFoo\nq\x00)\x81q\x01.'
>>> 
```
Hmmm… not good. So the above is probably a pretty compelling use case to change the behavior dill exhibits for classes defined in modules -- or at least enable one of the settings to provide better behavior.

issue_body:

```
import dill

class A(object):

a = A()

res = dill.dumps(a)

issue_title: Multiprocess Failing to pickle with pyOpenSSL version 16
issue_body:
Hello!

I've written a wrapper around Gspread (which is itself a wrapper, I know) and am also using Google-api-python-client to interface with Google Drive for more generic drive operations.

Part of my goals with the wrapper is to set up process monitoring - so that calls to the Google Drive API can timeout or otherwise fail without halting the whole program.  Multiprocess is serving this purpose for me; essentially I'm just using it to monitor the process for 60 seconds and then abandon it if hasn't completed yet.

With pyOpenSSL version 0.13.1 installed, everything works great.

With pyOpenSSL version 16 installed, I get the following error:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/bin/syscheck", line 7285, in <module>
    grapper_log_system(SYS)
  File "/usr/bin/syscheck", line 7062, in grapper_log_system
    grapper.add_worksheet(spreadsheet, "Validations / MAC List", datalist)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/grapper-0.1-py2.7.egg/grapper/grapper.py", line 57, in wrapper
    return p.get()
  File "/usr/lib64/python2.7/site-packages/multiprocess/pool.py", line 567, in get
    raise self._value
multiprocess.pool.MaybeEncodingError: Error sending result: '<Worksheet 'Validations / MAC List' id:o689mba>'. Reason: 'PicklingError("Can't pickle <type '_cffi_backend.CDataGCP'>: it's not found as _cffi_backend.CDataGCP",)'
```

I've tested pyOpenSSL versions 0.15.1, 0.15, and 0.14, and they all fail in the same way.

```
[BurnC7 (2016-06-24 11:17:48) grapper]# pip install pyOpenSSL==99999
Collecting pyOpenSSL==99999
  Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement pyOpenSSL==99999 (from versions: 0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 0.10, 0.12, 0.13, 0.13.1, 0.14, 0.15, 0.15.1, 16.0.0)
No matching distribution found for pyOpenSSL==99999
```

Is there something we can do about this, or do I need to be posting at pyOpenSSL?

issue_title: Suggest jvm missing dependencies
issue_body:
We recently enabled strict_deps for java, in doing so, we had an offline script that matches class not found errors from javac and look up their matching dependencies from transitive classpath.

This issue is to upstream this missing dependency recommendation fuction. It will be similar to the existing unused deps check, instead of calling on successful compile but on failed compiles.

issue_title: Copy payload.platform from java_thrift_library target to synthetic java_library target
issue_body:
Currently, scrooge.gen task won't copy payload.platform from java_thritft_library target to synthetic java_library target. In the case of platform change on java_thrift_library, cache_key for java_library artifact will not be invalidated, thus causing wrong artifacts get retrieved.

As a fix, when creating synthetic java_library target in scrooge.gen task, copy payload.platform from java_thrift_library.

issue_title: PANTS_CONFIG_FILES option behaves differently when passed str vs. list
issue_body:
This repro is using `--pants-config-files` - I know that this flag is a privileged one. Perhaps it is limited to just this repro but it may be a larger issue.

Here is a gist of the repro: https://gist.github.com/mateor/f1db0ab00c306eca2d8aa5d1c1008349

But the general idea is
a) rename `pants.ini` to `renamed.ini` or something.
b) invoking `PANTS_CONFIG_FILES=renamed.ini ./pants test contrib/scrooge::` fails with `No such file or directory: u'/Users/mateo/dev/pants/pants.ini'`
c) passing the same option as a list succeeds: `PANTS_CONFIG_FILES="['renamed.ini']" ./pants contrib/scrooge` runs and passes.

The same behavior occurs with passing it as a flag, `--pants-config-files=renamed.ini` returns the same error message. Except that `--pants-config-files="['renamed.ini']" test contrib/scrooge::` runs, but fails to pass the tests. It fails because the `pants_run` method chokes on the missing pants.ini file. The `pants_run` passes along environmental variables but only includes select command line flags.

Probably the `pants_run` method needs to consume any `--pants-config` or `--config-overrides` options and pass them in the invocation. I could not get that to work in my testing due to what appears to be complications passing the formatting to subprocess. But that is a followup issue.

If you try and repro this issue - you might hit some other weirdness with pants_run and the buildcache. I am working on an issue/PR for that now.

issue_title: In pants goal idea, some users are seeing ivy failures trying to download a -sources jar that does not exist.
issue_body:
This issue started with a discussion in https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/pants-devel/xfrYj0_UB-c

Square contributed a change to the ivy_resolve and ide_gen steps that pulls in 'sources' and 'javadoc' confs automatically when the user tries `./pants idea <target>`

When this feature was initially proposed, there was a concern noted in  https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/716/ that sometimes sources or javadocs might not be available and downloads would fail.  We wrote an [integration test](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/aca4aab4e081ffb0b9d1895e9922f359bb42da1f/tests/python/pants_test/tasks/test_idea_integration.py#L211) to show that Ivy would ignore missing sources or javadoc downloads.  

Usually, this feature works for us, but some users have seen a ivy get 'stuck' trying to download a missing sources javadoc.    Here is the ivy output we were seeing from the problem:

...
org.apache.httpcomponents#httpclient;4.1.2 by[org.apache.httpcomponents#httpclient;4.3.6](https://jira.corp.squareup.com/browse/STWO-169?jql=project%20%3D%20STWO%20AND%20resolution%20%3D%20Unresolved%20AND%20component%20%3D%20%22Pants%20%2F%20Java%20Build%22%20ORDER%20BY%20priority%20DESC#httpclient;4.3.6) in [default]
---------------------------------------------------------------------

 modules
artifacts
confnumbersearchdwnldedevicted
numberdwnlded

---------------------------------------------------------------------

default1770022
1550
sources105000
1020
javadoc105000
970

---------------------------------------------------------------------
:: problems summary ::
:::: WARNINGS
[NOT FOUND ] log4j#apache-log4j-extras;1.2.17!apache-log4j-extras.jar(source) (564ms)
==== remote-repos-square-maven: tried
[https://nexus.corp.squareup.com/content/groups/public/log4j/apache-log4j-extras/1.2.17/apache-log4j-extras-1.2.17-sources.jar](https://nexus.corp.squareup.com/content/groups/public/log4j/apache-log4j-extras/1.2.17/apache-log4j-extras-1.2.17-sources.jar)
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:: FAILED DOWNLOADS ::
:: ^ see resolution messages for details ^ ::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:: log4j#apache-log4j-extras;1.2.17!apache-log4j-extras.jar(source)
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
```

Then Ivy returns non-zero to the shell causing pants to exit.

To work around this problem, I submitted  https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/1939/ so that these users could run goal idea without requesting javadoc and sources.  This works, but we would like something more elegant.

I added more debugging and saw that the user's install is actually going out to our nexus and getting a 404 returned for this jar file.  I tried to reproduce using the same ivy.xml and same ivysettings.xml from inside of square and my installation didn't have any problems.  Then I moved the ivy cache out of the way and still didn't have any problems starting over from an empty cache.  The user reported moving ~/.ivy2 out of the way and still running into the issue.

issue_title: CI failing due to google-java-format quietly running before lint
issue_body:
## Background

See #5614 and https://travis-ci.org/pantsbuild/pants/jobs/356953064 for context.

The CI was failing due to a lint error on a java file that was not edited in the PR. Meanwhile the linter passes on the file as is when run locally. 

It turns out that CI is running google-java-format before running the linter, and google-java-format is rewriting the file in a way that causes the linter to fail.

## Solution?

I think the immediate solution here is to disable google-java-format by default. A long term suggestion might be to add an option for the formatter itself to exit non-zero if an edit is made, so that a formatter could be more programmatically integrated into a CI pipeline.  Obviously since the formatter caused the linter to fail, this is probably not a good idea until we update the google-java-format config to better match the project and linter style guidelines.

issue_title: Policy not applied
issue_body:
When I create a lambda with a statement like the following the policy is not applied but no error is thrown either. The result is a policy with only the `AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole` without any Role policies.

```yaml
  SignAttachmentUploadFunction:
    Type: 'AWS::Serverless::Function'
    Properties:
      Handler: 'index.handler'
      Runtime: 'nodejs4.3'
      CodeUri: 'lambdas/sign-attachment-upload'
      Description: !Sub '${Project} API endpoint for signing a new file upload with a random filename hash.'
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: 'AllowAttachmentUploads'
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: 'Allow'
                Action:
                  - 's3:PutObject'
                  - 's3:PutObjectACL'
                Resource: !Sub 'arn:aws:s3:::${AttachmentsBucket}/*'
      Environment:
        Variables:
          AttachmentUploadBucket: !Ref 'AttachmentsBucket'
      Events:
        SignAttachmentUpload:
          Type: 'Api'
          Properties:
            Path: '/sign-attachment-upload'
            Method: 'post'
    DependsOn:
      - 'AttachmentsBucket'
```

I solved it by only including the `PolicyDocument` instead of the entire policy.

```yaml
  SignAttachmentUploadFunction:
    Type: 'AWS::Serverless::Function'
    Properties:
      Handler: 'index.handler'
      Runtime: 'nodejs4.3'
      CodeUri: 'lambdas/sign-attachment-upload'
      Description: !Sub '${Project} API endpoint for signing a new file upload with a random filename hash.'
      Policies:
        - Version: '2012-10-17'
          Statement:
            - Effect: 'Allow'
              Action:
                - 's3:PutObject'
                - 's3:PutObjectACL'
              Resource: !Sub 'arn:aws:s3:::${AttachmentsBucket}/*'
      Environment:
        Variables:
          AttachmentUploadBucket: !Ref 'AttachmentsBucket'
      Events:
        SignAttachmentUpload:
          Type: 'Api'
          Properties:
            Path: '/sign-attachment-upload'
            Method: 'post'
    DependsOn:
      - 'AttachmentsBucket'
```

Thanks!

issue_title: Add support for "Body" param in AWS::Serverless::Api
issue_body:
If you want to leverage additional capabilities of API gateway (i.e CORS support, mock requests, etc) you'll need to create a swagger doc.  The only option right now is to create a swagger file, upload it to S3, and reference it via the `DefinitionUri` template.

As #8 alludes to, if you want to share data, you'll have to coordinate the `variables` section in `AWS::Serverless::Api` with the `${stageVariables.<foo>}` in the swagger file.

It would be really helpful to support the `Body` param from http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-resource-apigateway-restapi.html.

Benefits:

1. Can leverage all the cloudformation functions within the swagger body.
2. Simplifies process for tools wanting to generate SAM templates.  Everything (except the lambda deployment package) can be in a single file vs. generating a swagger file, uploading to s3, generating a SAM template that references the swagger file.

issue_title: How to get a refresh_token?
issue_body:
Hi,

I'd like to get the `refresh_token` for the first time only so the app doesn't require subsequent authentication prompts (as this will be a script running in the background). 

It works the first time but after the token expires it gives an error:

```
 gauth.Refresh()
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/pydrive/auth.py", line 369, in Refresh
    raise RefreshError('No refresh_token found.'
pydrive.auth.RefreshError: No refresh_token found.Please set access_type of OAuth to offline.
```

This is the code handling the authentication:
```python
# Sort out the authentication...
gauth = GoogleAuth()

# Try to load saved client credentials
gauth.LoadCredentialsFile(os.path.dirname(os.path.realpath(__file__)) + os.sep + "credentials.json")

if gauth.credentials is None:
    # Authenticate if they're not there
    gauth.LocalWebserverAuth()
elif gauth.access_token_expired:
    # Refresh them if expired
    gauth.Refresh()
else:
    # Initialize the saved creds
    gauth.Authorize()
```

Then I have a `settings.yaml`:

```yaml
client_config_backend: settings
client_config:
  client_id: xxx
  client_secret: xxx

save_credentials: True
save_credentials_backend: file
save_credentials_file: <path_to_save_credentials.json>

get_refresh_token: True

oauth_scope:
  - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.file
  - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.install
```

I believe `client_secrets.json` is not required if you have `settings.yaml`, is that correct?

Any ideas why `refresh_token` is always `NULL` in the response `credentials.json`?

issue_title: Using bounds in places_nearby
issue_body:
In the places class, the function places_nearby uses location+radius to search for places nearby, but in the [places API docs](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/javascript/places#place_search_requests) it also documents using a LatLng bounding box as type 'google.maps.LatLngBounds' instead of a location and radius.  But in the places_nearby function location is required and is converted to a latlng in _places:

`    if location:
        params["location"] = convert.latlng(location)`

This makes it seemingly impossible to send a bounding box, as it is not a single lat long, and there is not a param for bounds.  Is there a way to send a bounds object to the API instead of a location + radius with this library?

issue_title: Add support for strictbounds in place autocomplete
issue_body:
https://developers.google.com/places/web-service/autocomplete

strictbounds — Returns only those places that are strictly within the region defined by location and radius. This is a restriction, rather than a bias, meaning that results outside this region will not be returned even if they match the user input.

I'd like to see a pass-through param for this and any other query params that may be added.

issue_title: Error while playing a specified radio station
issue_body:
An error occurs if the station "Alsterradio 106.8 Rock" is played. You can find this station in TunIn -> Location -> Europe -> Germany -> Hamburg.

Here is the error:

File "/home/s/projects/shSonos/server.sonos/soco/events.py", line 230, in do_NOTIFY
    variables = parse_event_xml(content)
  File "/home/s/projects/shSonos/server.sonos/soco/events.py", line 136, in parse_event_xml
    value = from_didl_string(value)[0]
  File "/home/s/projects/shSonos/server.sonos/soco/data_structures.py", line 78, in from_didl_string
    items.append(cls.from_element(elt))
  File "/home/s/projects/shSonos/server.sonos/soco/data_structures.py", line 385, in from_element
    title = really_unicode(title_elt.text)
  File "/home/s/projects/shSonos/server.sonos/soco/utils.py", line 30, in really_unicode
    raise ValueError('%s is not a string at all.' % in_string)

Because the argument in_string is None, the really_unicode function fails. The metadata given by the radio station is a little bit strange. E.g. the track title comes in this format "\"Beat It Von Michael Jackson\"". This could be the issue.

As a workaround, i check for a none value in really_unicode(). A hack, but it seems to work.

```
def really_unicode(in_string):
 
    if not in_string:
        return
    if type(in_string) is StringType:
        for args in (('utf-8',), ('latin-1',), ('ascii', 'replace')):
            try:
                in_string = in_string.decode(*args)
                break
            except UnicodeDecodeError:
                continue
    if type(in_string) is not UnicodeType:
        raise ValueError('%s is not a string at all.' % in_string)
    return in_string
```


Pfischi

issue_title: <Imp Enhancement> Learner username is not displayed on the top right corner of the browser on a Portrait mode in a tablet.
issue_body:
## Summary
Learner username is not displayed on the top right corner of the browser on a Portrait mode in a tablet.

When the tablet is key in landscape mode the learners can see their logged in username on the top right corner, but when you switch it to portrait mode learner's login ID is not visible and on top of that he/she cannot know who's ID he is logged in, as the username is not visible on the dropdown option on the top right corner.

**Important user story**
Its always good to have the learners name displayed all times at the top right corner, so that the learner is aware that he/she is using their own account. This is a big issue since we have't implemented the #2131 and because of this at-least 50% of the leaner end up progressing on the wrong student account. Some learners prefer using Portrait mode and some prefer Landscape mode, hence is enhancement is important.

## System information
 - Version: 0.4.5 and 0.5.0
 - Operating system: Ubuntu 14.04LTS
 - Browser: Chrome.

## How to reproduce
1. Login in a tablet on Landscape mode, notice the user name is clearly visible at the top right corner.
2. Now switch to Portrait mode and notice the username is not visible and not found in the dropdown too.
3. The learner has to got to profiles to know the username.

## Screenshots
On landscape mode:
![img_20170919_162516](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13453618/30801189-113b35f2-a200-11e7-891e-68111c2b5ea2.jpg)

On Portrait mode:
![img_20170919_162503](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13453618/30801195-1a3cdd40-a200-11e7-9d88-89d5f498ecb6.jpg)



## Real-life consequences (anything community should be aware of, for instance how it affects your deployment)
Learner's end up using the wrong user accounts most of the time. Consequence is  👎  :)

issue_title: Re-use connections to reduce overhead
issue_body:
The client is currently creating a new HTTPS connection on every call. When the call rate is high, we could save a significant amount of overhead by keeping a connection open and reusing it, or even using a pool of connections.

https://github.com/castle/castle-python/blob/809e764f4b44023acd75fa6ac7370291f7618fd1/castle/request.py#L11-L12

I would consider a way to either pass in a request session, or have the client manage it. 

See http://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/api/#request-sessions

issue_title: Updating a Service in reality recreates it.
issue_body:
When trying to update a Service using the update() call, all existing attributes of the Service are reset.

For instance, given a Service that has Env, Mounts defined, performing a Service.update(image=new_image) will result in a service with the updated image definition, but without the previously existing Env and Mounts definitions.

This means that update() currently behaves as if you were to perform a Service.delete(), Service.create(). I would expect Service.update() to "copy" the existing Service definition and apply my changes.

In my specific case this is important because my service has replicas, deleting the service and recreating it (the current observed behavior) completely defeats the benefits of having a replica in the first place, as deleting the Service will destroy all running replicas at once.

issue_title: inconsistency in courses downloading/on-demand issues
issue_body:
There are several courses I've tried to download that fail.  I switched to including the cookies option (-c) and that helped for 2 courses, but several others remain.

The documentation states to use the option "--on-demand" for on demand courses.  When you use that option, it states that "--on-demand" has been deprecated.  There are two on-demand courses that the url looks incorrect when coursera-dl tries to access them.  

For the courser intro to public speaking, the URL is:
https://www.coursera.org/learn/public-speaking/home/welcome

And this is what coursera-dl tries to access:
https://class.coursera.org/public-speaking/lecture

It's trying to access an on-demand course with a not on-demand URL and gives a 404 error.  But there is no way to tell it that it's an on-demand course.

I have the same issue with the course what-is-social:
https://www.coursera.org/learn/what-is-social/home/welcome

issue_title: Stable, public API
issue_body:
We branch off a 0.10 maintenance branch, starting from 0.10.6. We will maintain this branch for some time with security and bug fixes, but no new features.

On the main branch, we now make more radical cleanups.

All vars  follow PEP8 naming conventions (all lower case, use of `_` as part separator) and all vars which are not part of the public API begin with a leading `_` ("private vars").

This will likely break things in at least:
- Crossbar.io
- AutobahnTestsuite

In AutobahnTestsuite, it's fine to require the 0.10 branch releases. In Crossbar.io we will track the new master.

The public API is described using type classes and meta classes in two new files:
- `autobahn/wamp/api.py`
- `autobahn/websocket/api.py`

Core interfaces are
- `IWebSocketProtocol` and  `IWebSocketMessage`
- `IWampTransport`, `IWampTransportHandler` and `IWampMessage`
- `IWampSession` and `IWampConnection`

All other types and interfaces are built around above.

issue_title: Wrong rest handling in time signature change
issue_body:
It's simpler try this little code than explain the issue :)

```python
from music21 import stream, note, duration, meter
m1 = stream.Measure()
m1.append(note.Note('a'))
m1.append(note.Note('b'))
m1.append(note.Note('c'))
m1.append(note.Note('d'))
m1.number = 1

m0 = stream.Measure()
m0.append(note.Note('e', duration = duration.Duration(0.5)))
m0.append(note.Note('f', duration = duration.Duration(0.5)))
m0.number = 0
m0.timeSignature = meter.TimeSignature('4/4')

s = stream.Stream()
s.append(m0)
s.append(m1)

# s is a stream with anacrusis incipit:
print('orginal: ')
s.show('png')

# filling first measure of s with rests:

r = note.Rest()
r.duration.quarterLength = 3

for e in s.measure(0).recurse().notesAndRests:
    e.offset += r.duration.quarterLength

s.measure(0).insert(0, r)

print('padded: ')
s.show('png')

# changing time signature...
s.measure(0).timeSignature = meter.TimeSignature('1/4')

print('changed signature: ')
s.show('png')

print('after make measures: ')
s.makeMeasures().show('png')
```
Output is the following:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22996003/33524515-c2b0e3e6-d81e-11e7-9388-736003867d3a.png)

If you try with '2/4' instead of '1/4' and with 3 rests of 1.0 quarter length the issue persists and elements inserted at offset _**x**_ of a measure will be at offset _**x**_ of the whole stream after `makeMeasures()`. I think that the problem is with `insert` function.

start = time.time()
with tp.PandasHDFStore('/path/to/my/store.h5') as s:
   tp.batch(frames, 5, minmass=2, percentile=10, invert=False, characterize=False, output=s)
end = time.time()
print (end-start)
start = time.time()
with tp.PandasHDFStore('/path/to/my/store.h5') as s:
   for linked in tp.link_df_iter(s, 0.4, memory=5):
       s.put(linked)
end = time.time()
print (end-start)
```

The code made it through the feature finding, ok and then memory usage built during linking.
Using anaconda (py2.7) we're updated to the latest pandas, numba, trackpy, etc...

issue_title: In pipeline, accept a JSON file as a data source
issue_body: Currently accepts CSV. JSON data sources are useful too.

issue_title: permille sign (‰) shouldn't be handled the same as percent sign (%)
issue_body:
### reproduction
* try to parse number field with value `5000‰`

#### expected
* treat it is a permile sign - parts per thousand, see - https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E2%80%B0#English
  * cast value should be `5` (5000 / 1000)
* spec only supports percent sign, so maybe it should be treated as invalid

#### actual
* treated as percent sign
  * cast value is `50` (5000 / 100)

### see also
* https://github.com/frictionlessdata/tableschema-py/blob/master/tests/types/test_number.py#L23

issue_title: Improve behavior of error reporting
issue_body:
Currently by default running DSC on a local computer hides stderr and stdout. We should write them to files and point users to them when there are errors. We need to also clean them up if they turn out to be empty to prevent leaving behind us a trail of trivial err and out files. 

Also we need to make the rerun prompt `Rscript <path to script>` more obvious -- even suggest in the prompt that users should open up the file and interact with it.

issue_title: Add driver config option to disable write_memory call every time.
issue_body: `disable_memory_write` - default to False, allow setting to True to disable the memory write in a10_context.

issue_title: SVERI may no longer have latest data
issue_body: `SVERIClient.get_generation(latest=True)` returns no data after switching to the new url.

issue_title: Add custom external tool setup callbacks
issue_body:
In multi-user environment, it's desired to keep single instance of expensive to install tools (e.g. Ruby, Node.js).
CID already supports "external tool install" which assumes all tools are pre-installed.
It should be possible to provide environmental callback command for "cid tool install {name} [{ver}]", "cid tool update {name} [{ver}]" and any other identified in analysis.

Hi,

This project is quite new to me but it seems that I'll be hanging around here for a while. ;) I encounter some strange issue and I'm not sure if this is a desired behavior.

We have several users on our deis platform that including 3 admins (with superuser privilages). If some of them add application certificate it can't be listed or removed by another admin.

First admin (who added certificates):

```
$ deis certs:list
Common Name                Expires
-------------------------  ----------------------
*.myawesomecert.com                  2016-08-24T23:59:59UTC

```
$ deis perms:list --admin
{
  "count": 3,

issue_title: Coreos user-data file uses public ip for fleet, firewall script brings fleet down
issue_body:
Tested on Deis 1.11.

User-data example file in contrib/coreos/user-data example is using public ip for fleet. As etcd works with private ip, applying custom_firewall.sh on digital ocean causes fleet to fail with etcd errors.

I was able to work around this, by replacing
```
fleet:
    public-ip: $public_ipv4
```
with 
```
fleet:
    public-ip: $private_ipv4
```

issue_title: deis-router error_log should have etcd configurable error level
issue_body:
When debugging issues with `deis-router` it might be difficult to track the error down since we are relying on the default logging level in `error_log` - `error`. While there are log levels going all the way down to `debug`. [ngx_core_module#error_log](http://nginx.org/en/docs/ngx_core_module.html#error_log)

We already compile with `--with-debug`. So all we need to do is expose the ability to change `error_log` via a `etcd` key.

(This should be a quick 2 line fix. One in nginx.conf and one in docs.)

issue_title: Add `--notify` option to Transfer Task submission
issue_body:
The Transfer API supports notification settings whenever a Task is submitted:
`notify_on_succeeded`, `notify_on_failed`, and `notify_on_inactive`

Exposing this in the CLI could go down a number of routes, but here's one option:
`--notify off`: no notifications, all set to False
`--notify on`: (default) all set to True
`--notify "succeeded,failed"`: don't notify when tasks become inactive
`--notify failed`: notify on when tasks fail
`--notify "off,succeeded"`: UsageError
`--notify "succeeded,failed,inactive"`: same as default, but explicitly set these to True (vs. omitting from API doc)

I don't want to get too fancy about having options which are, themselves, parsed (vs. `--notify` being an option where we allow multiple usage), but `--notify off --notify failed` seems inherently ambiguous. Not allowing multiple uses and following `click`'s typical "last use wins" logic 

Relates to globusonline/koa#62

issue_title: Consolidate APIs to select peers from which to request data
issue_body:
We have several APIs for selecting a peer when we want to fetch data: `LightPeerChain.get_best_peer()`, `PeerPool.get_random_peer()`, `PeerPool.peers`, `StateDownloader.get_idle_peer()` and a couple places in `FastChainSyncer` that sort/filter `PeerPool.peers` based on some criteria. 

Apart from `StateDownloader.get_idle_peer()`, I think we should be able to have a single API on `PeerPool` that returns a peer known to have a certain block (defined by its number or TD). That would avoid duplication and ensure we always request data to peers known to have them.

issue_title: Decouple `Chain` from networking
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

The `evm.p2p.lightchain.LightChain` extends the `Chain` class.  This is looking to be problematic as I implement the various processes in `trinity`.  The JSON-RPC server needs access to the `Chain` object.   In order to do that as things are written, we'd need to setup a `multiprocessing.Manager` in the networking process to expose the various chain methods for the JSON-RPC process.

This is problematic because it allows for requests to the JSON-RPC server to compete with the execution of the networking processes.

### How can it be fixed

I would like to create a new class *type* to contain the networking specific code that currently lives in the `LightChain` class.  Maybe something like `ChainNetwork` or `NetworkManager`.  This class can then interact with the `Chain` using a proxy object, much the same way that it interacts with the `ChainDB` object.

issue_title: Implement gas estimation
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

We need to implement gas estimation for the JSON-RPC interface.

An ideal gas estimation API should take into account the following.

- total gas used isn't always right due to gas refunds.
- transactions can alter execution flow based on the txn gas.

### How can it be fixed

This should be implemented using a swappable backend API.  Accurate gas estimations are likely to be computationally expensive where-as naive estimations based on gas used are likely to work for most use cases and should be computationally cheap.

The API can be something as simple as a function which takes `VM` instance and a `Transaction` instance and returns the gas estimation.  It *might* be appropriate to include this as an API on the `Chain` and `VM` as `Chain.estimate_transaction_gas()` and `VM.estimate_transaction_gas()`

#### Strategies

We should first implement a `PeekGasUsedStrategy` which uses the peek gas usage

1. run the computation with the gas limit set at the full gas limit for the next pending block.
2. return the `computation.gas_meter.start_gas - computation.gas_metea.gas_remaining` for the outer computation.

Then we should implement what I believe is the current strategy used by go-ethereum which starts with running the transaction using the gas limit for the next pending block and then performs a binary search, re-running the transaction at each level of the search until it finds the threshold for which the transaction succeeds/fails.

> Note that the binary search can be performed at a higher granularity than 1 gas.  From @carver comment, a 1024 granularity should produce approximately a 2x speedup.

issue_title: Re-organize networking code for Trinity
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

Currently, all of the networking code lives under `evm.p2p`.  The *long* term plan is to split the core DevP2P components from this code into a separate library (probably the existing `devp2p` python library).  To prepare for this, we should start drawing firmer lines between the different sections of code that currently live in this repository.

1. Py-EVM code: deals specifically with the EVM
2. DevP2P code: deals with the generic DevP2P protocol
3. Trinity code: deals with running an Ethereum node over the DevP2P protocol using the `py-evm` EVM.



### How can it be fixed

Perform the following code re-organization.  This should be done in multiple steps to keep the pull requests smaller.

1. Everything from `evm.p2p` to a new root level module `devp2p`
2. Move everything from the new `devp2p` module which is not *strictly* DevP2P protocol code to `trinity.p2p`
3. Find *any* imports in either of these sections of code which import from `evm.utils.*` and change them to use their own local utilities, **or** migrate the utility to `eth-utils` so it can be shared.

issue_title: Issues with conda behind proxy (without username/password)
issue_body:
I am using Windows 64bit computer with:

```
>conda --version
conda 4.1.4
>python --version
Python 3.5.1 :: Anaconda 4.1.0 (64-bit)
```
#### With .condarc proxy

I have set my conda proxy at `.condarc`:

```
proxy_servers:
    http: http://proxy.example.com:8888
    https: https://proxy.example.com:8888
```

I then try to update a package in conda:

```
>conda update nb_conda_kernels
Fetching package metadata ...
https proxy username: <press enter as I don't have a username>

https proxy username: Password: <press enter as I don't have a password>

https proxy username: Password: <just keep pressing enter a few times>
```
#### Trying to manually download package

I even tried to copy the archive of the package into my `pkgs`  folder from http://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free/win-64/ and ran with `--unknown` as I saw in another issue. And it gives:

```
>conda update nb_conda_kernels --unknown
Fetching package metadata ...
https proxy username: <press enter as I don't have a username>

https proxy username: Password:<press enter as I don't have a password>

https proxy username: Password: <just keep pressing enter a few times>
Could not connect to https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free/win-64/
..
```

Also, after I enter the username/password twice, it just hangs. Doesnt respond to Ctrl+C, ctrl+z, etc. and to stop it I have to close the cmd prompt.
#### With HTTP_PROXY env variables.

I also have HTTP_PROXY and HTTPS_PROXY set up in the environment variables. When I remove those lines in .condarc and try it, it says:

```
>conda update nb_conda_kernels
Fetching package metadata ...Could not find a proxy for 'https'. See
http://conda.pydata.org/docs/html#configure-conda-for-use-behind-a-proxy-server
for more information on how to configure proxies.
```
#### Issues
1. I am not sure why it asks me for the password to begin with. Git, pip, etc do not ask me for the password and works fine.
2. I am not sure why it is not using HTTP_PROXY and HTTPS_PROXY as environment variables.
3. I am not sure why I can't kill the stuck conda operation with Ctrl-C.

issue_title: Activate environment not working on windows
issue_body:
This morning I installed Anaconda 2.3 with Python 3 on Windows 7 64-bit.
Then I created a Python 2.7 environment in the usual way.
Then I activated it "activate py27" and received a message that it was activated.
Then I ran "python -V" and it reports Python 3.4, not Python 2.7.

Other people have also had this problem, e.g. Shantnu's comment on this page: http://pythonforengineers.com/python-on-windows-causing-you-pain-try-anaconda/

Hi,

$ patchelf --version
patchelf 0.9
$ file a.out
a.out: ELF 64-bit LSB executable, x86-64, version 1 (GNU/Linux), statically linked, for GNU/Linux 2.6.32, BuildID[sha1]=5409b9978f2e5a5155090e1ac3d656f282848ad1, not stripped

cannot find section .dynamic
$ echo $?
1

See the travis build log at https://s3.amazonaws.com/archive.travis-ci.org/jobs/163653211/log.txt for an example

This seems to be a regression in conda-build, (it was working until a few days ago) but I haven't had opportunity yet to track down the change.

issue_title: conda install -f broken (when redownloading) in 4.0.0
issue_body:
So @kalefranz, I found the issue!  The problem is because of the new code starting at
https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/master/conda/api.py#L44
When the exact same package is updated on the server, the metadata still comes from the local metadata in the `<PREFIX>/conda-meta/` directory.  This is really bad, because then the download will fail with an `MD5 mismatch error`, because the new package on the server has a new MD5.

@mcg1969, I thought you told me the local data from `<PREFIX>/conda-meta/` wasn't used anymore, unless no official repodata was available.  This is not the case.  We need to fix this ASAP.

issue_title: Default channel documentation
issue_body:
I've been digging for 2 days to find information on how to add my package to the default channel so that a `conda install mypackage` will work without having to add my personal channel locally.

Is there any documentation that explains how PyQt, h5py and other similar libraries get a place in the default channel, and what requirements have to be there for other organizations/people to get it?

issue_title: conda install does not resolve pinned transitive dependencies
issue_body:
Consider the following scenario: my conda env already has package `A` at version `x`. I `conda install D`, which depends on `C`, which depends on `B`, which depends on `A` pinned at version `y > x`. There is no conflict of `A` version `y` with any other package.

`conda install D` does *not* upgrade `A`, leaving `D` in a broken state.

My concrete example involves packages on conda-forge (which I believe should not make a difference) and the packages in question are: `magics` dependens on `python-eccodes` depends on `jasper` depends on `jpeg 9.*`. The Azure notebooks root env provides `jpeg 8d`. `conda install -y -c conda-forge magics` does *not* upgrade `jpeg`.

This is using `conda` 4.3.22 on python 2.7. Azure notebooks currently provides the Anaconda 4.10 package set with some Azure specific extras.

issue_title: [Bug] Temporary files should be created at temporary dir
issue_body:
Reasons:
* maybe not possible to create them at the current file path (as seen below)
* if access is granted and something goes wrong you have temporary files that likely stay in a non temporary folder

```log
ERROR::pyqode.backend::something went bad with worker <function lint at 0x034C10C0>(data={'path': 'C:\\TEST.COB', 'code': [removed from log], 'encoding': 'cp1252'})
ERROR::pyqode.backend::Traceback (most recent call last):

ERROR::pyqode.backend::PermissionError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: 'C:\\.oci1433411286.cbl'
```

This is what cobc/libcob does (pseudocode):

```c
if !(tmpdir = getenv ("TMPDIR")) {
#ifdef	_WIN32
   if (tmpdir = !getenv ("TEMP"))
      if !(tmpdir = getenv ("TMP"))
         if !(tmpdir = getenv ("USERPROFILE"))
            tmpdir = .;
#else
   if (tmpdir = !getenv ("TMP"))
      if !(tmpdir = getenv ("TEMP"))
         tmpdir = /tmp;
#endif

   setenv("TMPDIR", tmpdir);
}
```

For supporting relative paths to copybooks it should be enough to pass an additional -I $fullpath_of_current_file to the compiler

issue_title: Weekly index 2017_1
issue_body:
Hello,

I use curator 4.0.5 and have the following actions file

```
actions:
  1:
    action: alias
    description: >-
      I can make the description span multiple
      lines by putting ">-" at the beginning of the line,
      as seen above.  Subsequent lines must also be indented.
    options:
      name: data_last_3_months
      extra_settings:
      timeout_override:
      continue_if_exception: False
      disable_action: False
    add:
      filters:
        - filtertype: pattern
          kind: prefix
          value: data_
          exclude:
        - filtertype: age
          source: name
          direction: older
          timestring: '%Y_%W'
          unit: weeks
          unit_count: -1
          exclude:
        - filtertype: age
          source: name
          direction: older
          timestring: '%Y_%W'
          unit: weeks
          unit_count: 12
          exclude: True
```

We already have indices `data_2016_52` and `data_2017_1`. However, as of today `data_2017_1` is not added into the alias `data_last_3_month`, even though today is already 2017. Our index is created based on `ISO 8601` ISO week.

What am I missing here. This year Jan 1st is on Sunday. But I'm running this on Jan 2nd, so the ISO week should not have an issue.

issue_title: Surrogates from poorly formed Unicode breaks transport layer
issue_body:
In any situation where you are ingesting content into ElasticSearch that you do not directly control, there is the possibility that someone somewhere along the lines has improperly encoded UTF-16 or another encoding to UTF-8 through the use of surrogate pairs.

These are not supported in UTF-8, in Python, or in ElasticSearch, and will cause mountains of errors like this:

```
File \"/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/elasticsearch/transport.py\", line 295, in perform_request
   body = body.encode('utf-8')
UnicodeEncodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't encode character '\udd9e' in position 19281: surrogates not allowed
```

The fix is quite simple, surrogate escapes must be backslash replaced in Python3:
```
try:
    body.encode("utf-8")
except UnicodeEncodeError as e:
    if e.reason == 'surrogates not allowed':
        body = body.encode('utf-8', "backslashreplace").decode('utf-8')
```

I will open a pull request with this fix.

issue_title: [Feature request] To download remote files into local without keeping the same virtual path of remote files but only file names
issue_body:
REQUEST:  to be able to download remote files into local without keeping the same virtual path of remote files but only file names

Here is a simple example:
```
<MyBlobStorage>: <MyContainer>/<SubDirectory1>/<SubDirectory2>/TargetFile.txt
<MyLocalVM>:/tmp
```

Suppose I want to download TargetFile.txt in blob storage to /tmp in my local VM, I run blobxfer by specifying remote path and local path with --remote-path and --local-path respectively like this:
```
blobxfer download --remote-path <MyContainer>/<SubDirectory1>/<SubDirectory2> --local-path /tmp ...
```
Then, blobxfer will attempt to download TargetFile.txt in blob storage to local VM with including path, keeping the same virtual file path like this:
```
<MyLocalVM>:/tmp/<SubDirectory1>/<SubDirectory2>/TargetFile.txt
```
But what I would like to achieve is simply to download files to local directory without keeping virtual path (virtual sub directory) like this:
```
<MyLocalVM>:/tmp/TargetFile.txt
```

Giving another example, 
Suppose I have the following 3 files in my Azure blob storage,
```
<MyBlobStorage>: <MyContainer>/<SubDirectory1>/<SubDirectory2>/TargetFile1.txt
<MyBlobStorage>: <MyContainer>/<SubDirectory1>/TargetFile2.txt
<MyBlobStorage>: <MyContainer>/TargetFile3.txt
```
I try to download the files above to my local VM /tmp directory by running the following command:
```
blobxfer download --remote-path <MyContainer> --local-path /tmp 
```
I would like to download files to local directory like this:
```
<MyLocalVM>$ ls -1  /tmp
TargetFile1.txt
TargetFile2.txt
TargetFile3.txt
```

issue_title: [DNS] Zone import progress report doesn't indicate overall progress
issue_body:
During zone import, I see a progress report listing each record imported.

The problem is that importing a large zone may take a long time, and I don't get any information that tells me how far I've got / how much is left to run.

Can we change the output to include current record number / total records?

E.g.

`Importing record 35,234 of 250,000, of type CNAME, record name "foo.bar.com"`

issue_title: implement `az acs list-locations`
issue_body:
Currently there is no way to figure out which data centers are able to create acs clusters. The only way to know right now as far as I know is to fail through trial-by-error, then use the regions listed in the error output. For example:

```
><> az acs create --resource-group="${AZURE_RG_NAME}" --location="canadaeast" --orchestrator-type=kubernetes --master-count=1 --agent-count=1 --agent-vm-size="Standard_D2_v2" --admin-username="k8sadmin" --name="${AZURE_SERVICE_NAME}" --dns-prefix="${AZURE_DNS_PREFIX}" --ssh-key-value @/home/bacongobbler/.ssh/id_rsa.pub 
waiting for AAD role to propagate.done
The provided location 'canadaeast' is not available for resource type 'Microsoft.ContainerService/containerServices'. List of available regions for the resource type is 'japaneast,centralus,eastus2,japanwest,eastasia,southcentralus,australiaeast,australiasoutheast,brazilsouth,southeastasia,westus,northcentralus,westeurope,northeurope,eastus'.
```

It would be great if there was a way to query which regions are available for the given resource type before creating the cluster. Right now it's a matter of picking one from `az account list-locations --query [].name --output tsv` and hoping it works.

issue_title: [Role] Assignment/Definition list should show "role name" in the default table output
issue_body: When using table-based output, the default response for the `az role definition list` and `az role assignment list` commands simply displays the role GUID, and not the "friendly names" (e.g. `Contributor`). Viewing JSON output, or running a query (e.g. `az role definition list --query "[].{name:name, roleName:properties.roleName}"`) allows working around this, but since other command's help text refer to role's by name (e.g. `az ad sp create-for-rbac`), it seems like it would be useful for the role lists to provide this value by default.

issue_title: Automation: Improve the package release process
issue_body:
Here are the notes I took during the past package release practice.

1. The versions are hardcoded in the source code. Therefore, in order to rev the packages version code changes were deliberately made to `setup.py` and `__init__.py`. It worth consider taking the value out of the source code and leverage environment variable or other sources to define version.
2. It requires too many release comments and pull requests. Make the bot to release multiple packages in one commit.
3. Can we rely on tag instead of a symbolic commit?
4. The bot relies on pull request merge event for trigger mechanism. It is not ideal for automation. It should rely on git merge event.
5. The bot release failed when pull requests were merged "too fast".
6. Command modules' versions are not aligned.
7. There isn't strong verification mechanism after package or bundle is released.
8. Package scripts are python 2.7 only.
9. The apt build can only be done on Ubuntu 14.04

issue_title: Open up the paging mechanism in list methods.
issue_body:
Take examples of [list vm](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/master/azure-mgmt-compute/azure/mgmt/compute/compute/v2017_03_30/operations/virtual_machines_operations.py#L635), and [list ad users](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/master/azure-graphrbac/azure/graphrbac/operations/users_operations.py#L99). In both operations, the internal paging mechanism travels the entire results before returning.The mechanism results in a poor performance when the number of the results is large.

An ideal change is to return results as it fetches data. For example, the list methods can return a generator instead of a list, which allows the consuming application to output the result as the SDK querying the data so as to offer better user experience.

An ultimate solution is to provide server side paging API by allowing `top` and `count` parameter in list function.

issue_title: lat/lon precision of 1cm, integer numbers where possible
issue_body:
Right now the code is emitting WGS 84 coordinates accurate to sub-micron scales. for instance, us-ca-nevada-county has locations like "-121.09431875581483,39.24202781459825". As much faith as I have in the Nevada County GIS department, that may be a bit optimistic. Coordinates should be rounded to 0.0000001 degree in output CSV (Python `%.7f`), which works out to about 1 centimeter. It will make the CSV files smaller.

In addition, some sources like us-ca-nevada-county also emit street addresses in decimal. That may be correct for 1/2 addresses, but for the majority of integers it'd be better to round. ie: the column should read "1337" and not "1337.0".

issue_title: jupyter notebook slows down after multiple executions and needs a kernel restart
issue_body:
Unfortunately, this error is not so easy to clearly reproduce and I'm not sure if it's a jupyter issue or an iPython kernel issue or maybe even due to the other libraries I'm using... But since it can be "fixed" by a kernel restart, I'm assuming it should be due to jupyter or the iPython kernel.

I'm training neural networks using the keras library on a Nvidia GPU on a local desktop computer running Linux Peppermint 8 (a derivative of ubuntu 17.4). I have jupyter notebook version 5.0.0 for python 2.7 installed via anaconda.

My problem is that at the beginning of executing the notebook, everything runs really fast and the neural networks train with less than 100ms per training epoch. After executing some cells multiple times, e.g. to test different parameters, after some time the training starts to slow down, taking at least twice as long and it gets worse the more often I run the cells. Then if I restart the kernel, when I execute all the cells again, everything is fast again.

I'm not really sure how this can happen. I assume it has something to do with the garbage collection (or whatever is being performed at a restart), but as I was saying I'm mostly reexecuting the same cells, not adding more, so it should overwrite the existing variables and not add stuff to the stack.

issue_title: Multiply-wrapped elements over-indented
issue_body:
Currently, the notebook wraps double-wrapped elements as:

    a = np.array([
            1,
            2,
        ])

That is, it doubles up the indentation.

Compare this to the behavior of PyCharm:

    a = np.array([
        1,
        2,
    ])

Sublime Text takes a third approach:

    a = np.array([
                 1,
                 2,
                 ])

As far as I can tell, PEP8 doesn't have a recommendation here, so I recognize this is ambiguous. Nevertheless, I find the PyCharm behavior ideal. To me, Sublime's behavior is the worst of the three, *However*, once you fix the second line (with the `1` in my examples), further newlines will start  at the same indentation level. In the notebook, even after adjusting the `1` line, the `2` line starts one level deeper. The indentation has to be fixed on every line. Even if there's no consensus as to which default behavior is best, ideally the current indentation should be picked up on.

Thoughts?

def consume_func(content):
    # do custom stuff with content
    time.sleep(5)
    return

def async_handler(message):
    global consume_tasks
    message.enable_async()

    job = threading.Thread(
        target=consume_func,
        args=(message.body.decode(),)
    )
    consume_tasks.append({
        'message': message,
        'job': job,
    })
    job.start()

def cleanup_loop():
    global consume_tasks
    while True:
        unfinished_tasks = []
        for task in consume_tasks:
            if not task.get('job').is_alive():
                task.get('message').finish()
            else:
                unfinished_tasks.append(task)
        consume_tasks = unfinished_tasks
        time.sleep(0.1)

reader = nsq.Reader(message_handler=handler, lookupd_http_addresses='',
                    topic='Test123', channel='async', max_in_flight=10)
threading.Thread(target=cleanup_loop).start()
nsq.run()

issue_title: [Enhancement] Easier Visualization
issue_body:
Hey there,

I think the mesa visualizations are quite neat, but are a bit clumsy to set up. I don't know what the exact problem was, but when I created my first chart viz I struggled quite a bit. I think this was partly because the set-up is quite un-pythonic. When I finally managed to get it to work, I was underwhelmed. Nothing I couldn't have done with matplotlib in less then 5 minutes. 

Therefore I propose we make the visualization part more Netlogo-ish ready to go and leave the details to be worked out programmatically. My ideas:

1. Create a visualization server with a one-liner (create grid viz by default, model parameters as user-settable)
2. Change limits of user-settable parameters in the visualization
3. Allow to add visualizations interactively (see #440 )
4. Drag-and-Drop visualization elements and save layout
5. Create more default visualization elements (e.g. histograms)

I think this could greatly improve the usability for novice users of mesa. Fine-grain control should of course still be remained as is now, but I see the Viz element more as a playground for the model. I don't want to spend a lot of time with it when I need to use different tools anyway to create publication-ready graphs. 

What you think?

Summary:

After an attempt at installation, invoking androgui.py gave the error "No PyQT5 found! Exiting..." when it was pyperclip that was missing.

Expectations:

I expected the gui to start or to be given a different error message :)

What Happened:

What actually happened was the program exited and a misleading error message was displayed.  (But at least it wasn't too hard to manually execute lines from androgui.py to get a better error message which helped to understand the situation more accurately.)  Installing pyperclip addressed the issue.  Searching the repository for pyperclip turned up a few references to it being a dependency, but these references were only in the issues -- I didn't succeed in finding pyperclip mentioned as a dependency in the documentation.

System Information:

- Androguard Version: master
- Python Version: 3.6.4
- Operating System: Manjaro Linux

issue_title: Allow filtering report by test outcome

issue_title: xdist does not work with --assert=plain
issue_body:
Running 
pytest --doctest-modules -v --assert=plain 
works without assert rewriting. 

Running 
pytest --doctest-modules -v --assert=plain -n 2
is still doing the assert rewriting. 

I have to use --assert=plain as the tests are using pickle and assert rewriting is creating problems with pickle. Specifically it tries to pickle typing.io which does not support pickling.

issue_title: Offer Tracking for Stacked Offers
issue_body:
I was testing out the stacked offer tracking that will be included in v1.6 and came across what may be a bug. It looks like the `LineOfferConsumer` class might be tracking the applied offers incorrectly.

As an example, in the basket app when  `AbstractLine.consume()` runs it should mark a line as consumed using `LineOfferConsumer.consume()` and passing the applicable offer. I think it should be storing the offer that consumed the items regardless of if the offer was exclusive or not. However, if the offer is exclusive it doesn't store the offer that consumed the line items.

This is for a test basket with an offer that should be applied to all three items in the line:

```python
class Line(AbstractLine):

    def consume(self, quantity, offer=None):
        super(Line, self).consume(quantity, offer)
        print "offer------------------------->", offer
        print "exclusive--------------------->", offer.exclusive
        print "has_discount------------------>", self.has_discount
        print "quantity_with_discount-------->", self.quantity_with_discount
        print "has_offer_discount------------>", self.has_offer_discount(offer)
        print "quantity_with_offer_discount-->", self.quantity_with_offer_discount(offer)
```

Output:

```
offer-------------------------> Discount 123456
exclusive---------------------> True
has_discount------------------> True
quantity_with_discount--------> 3
has_offer_discount------------> False
quantity_with_offer_discount--> 0
```

If the offer is set to non-exclusive then the behavior is as expected:

```
offer-------------------------> Discount 123456
exclusive---------------------> False
has_discount------------------> True
quantity_with_discount--------> 3
has_offer_discount------------> True
quantity_with_offer_discount--> 3
```

I don't actually need stacked offers per se, I just need to know what offer was applied to the line as a whole. Maybe this is stored somewhere else when a whole line is consumed by the same offer?

issue_title: Need little help how to sort products in a category by 2 fields?
issue_body:
In `StockReckords` there are products, which can be provided by few partners (lets say for now Partner # 1 is our remote partner with a remore warehouse and Partner # 2 is our shop with our warehouse)

At front-end I want to show just products which our warehouse has.

I tried
```
from oscar.apps.catalogue.search_handlers import SolrProductSearchHandler as SProductSearchHandler

class SolrProductSearchHandler(SProductSearchHandler):

    def get_search_queryset(self):
        sqs = super(SolrProductSearchHandler, self).get_search_queryset()
        return sqs.filter(partner='name_of_our_shop').order_by('-num_in_stock')
```
But Solr, at the front-end, does not sort products correctly. Solr when does ordering by `num_in_stock` takes into account not JUST `num_in_stock` of our shop, but also takes into account `num_in_stock` of other partners. In other words - Solr starts to show just those products, which has relation to partner # 2 (our shop), but takes `num_in_stock` from Partner # 1.

For example, there is a product Intel i5-5200, Partner # 1 has 20 items, we have 0. I expect to get this product at the end of the page, but I see it at the top.

How to restict sorting by Partner # 2 (our shop) + our `num_in_stock`?

Thanks a lot!

issue_title: Version must be set on CSR before marshaling
issue_body:
If you happen to have a very old version of OpenSSL (looking at you Travis) and you don't set the CSR Version an empty integer (0 length byte slice) will be inserted into the version field which causes Boulder to choke as this is an invalid DER encoding. On newer versions if the version isn't set OpenSSL will sneakily just insert a `0` value and continue, meaning we hadn't stumbled across this before.

The version should be set using `req.set_version(2)` (2 = X509v3... because) in [`make_csr`](https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/blob/fd1ebb2d9f6a0030307028aad92ceee586fc06c5/letsencrypt/crypto_util.py#L95). Fixing this will allow us to unblock moving Boulder to Golang 1.6 (which we should build a Boulder instance from and run a test-farm thingies against just to check!)
