issue_title: provide systemd service template for pypiserver
issue_body:
Any daemon is supposed to run as a service, thus pypi-server needs a `systemd` template in order to be used as a proper services on Debian distributions (including Ubuntu).

If nobody has an already made template I will create one and contribute it.

Once this is installed people should be able to contron th pypi-server using commands line
```
service pypi-server start
service pypi-server stop
systemctl enable pypi-server
systemctl disable pypi-server
```
More information about systemd can be found at https://www.digitalocean.com/community/tutorials/how-to-use-systemctl-to-manage-systemd-services-and-units

server_nonce = server_nonce.to_bytes(16, 'little', signed=True)
```
A `bytes` object has no `to_bytes` attribute, so either the test function or the helper function could be changed to fix this. The test would probably be easier to change, since it would break less.

If I've missed something obvious, sorry -- I'm not familiar with filing issues. If this is a concern, I can open a PR also.

issue_title: CWLtool issue with passing a list of scatter/gathered outputs into a program that accepts a list
issue_body:
This works:

```
#!/usr/bin/env cwl-runner
class: Workflow
requirements:
  - class: ScatterFeatureRequirement
inputs:
  - id: "#mutation"
    type: string
  - id: "#normalin"
    type: File
  - id: "#tumorin"
    type: File
outputs:
  - id: "#outfile"
    type: File
    source: "#wc.outfile"
steps:
  - id: "#greptumor"
    run: {import: grep.cwl.yaml}
    #scatter: "#grep.infile"
    inputs:
      - id: "#grep.pattern"
        source: "#mutation"
      - id: "#grep.infile"
        source: "#tumorin"
    outputs:
      - id: "#greptumor.outfile"
  - id: "#grepnormal"
    run: {import: grep.cwl.yaml}
    #scatter: "#grep.infile"
    inputs:
      - id: "#grep.pattern"
        source: "#mutation"
      - id: "#grep.infile"
        source: "#normalin"
    outputs:
      - id: "#grepnormal.outfile"
  - id: "#wc"
    run: {import: wc.cwl.yaml}
    inputs:
      - id: "#wc.infile"
        source: ["#grepnormal.outfile",  "#greptumor.outfile"]
    outputs:
      - id: "#wc.outfile"
```

This does not work, when I try to have it work on multiple normal/tumor input files into grep scattered:

```
#!/usr/bin/env cwl-runner
class: Workflow
requirements:
  - class: ScatterFeatureRequirement
inputs:
  - id: "#mutation"
    type: string
  - id: "#normalin"
    type: {type: array, items: File}
  - id: "#tumorin"
    type: {type: array, items: File}
outputs:
  - id: "#outfile"
    type: File
    source: "#wc.outfile"
steps:
  - id: "#greptumor"
    run: {import: grep.cwl.yaml}
    scatter: "#grep.infile"
    inputs:
      - id: "#grep.pattern"
        source: "#mutation"
      - id: "#grep.infile"
        source: "#tumorin"
    outputs:
      - id: "#greptumor.outfile"
  - id: "#grepnormal"
    run: {import: grep.cwl.yaml}
    scatter: "#grep.infile"
    inputs:
      - id: "#grep.pattern"
        source: "#mutation"
      - id: "#grep.infile"
        source: "#normalin"
    outputs:
      - id: "#grepnormal.outfile"
  - id: "#wc"
    run: {import: wc.cwl.yaml}
    inputs:
      - id: "#wc.infile"
        source: ["#grepnormal.outfile",  "#greptumor.outfile"]
    outputs:
      - id: "#wc.outfile"
```


Interestingly though, this small change does work, when you only pass one of the scatter/gathered inputs into the final wc.

```
#!/usr/bin/env cwl-runner
class: Workflow
requirements:
  - class: ScatterFeatureRequirement
inputs:
  - id: "#mutation"
    type: string
  - id: "#normalin"
    type: {type: array, items: File}
  - id: "#tumorin"
    type: {type: array, items: File}
outputs:
  - id: "#outfile"
    type: File
    source: "#wc.outfile"
steps:
  - id: "#greptumor"
    run: {import: grep.cwl.yaml}
    scatter: "#grep.infile"
    inputs:
      - id: "#grep.pattern"
        source: "#mutation"
      - id: "#grep.infile"
        source: "#tumorin"
    outputs:
      - id: "#greptumor.outfile"
  - id: "#grepnormal"
    run: {import: grep.cwl.yaml}
    scatter: "#grep.infile"
    inputs:
      - id: "#grep.pattern"
        source: "#mutation"
      - id: "#grep.infile"
        source: "#normalin"
    outputs:
      - id: "#grepnormal.outfile"
  - id: "#wc"
    run: {import: wc.cwl.yaml}
    inputs:
      - id: "#wc.infile"
        source: ["#grepnormal.outfile"]
    outputs:
      - id: "#wc.outfile"
```

So it seems to me that this is an issue only when you use the program accepting an array of inputs and you want to pass two of those scatter/gathered things into a final output. Maybe the data structure being output in the scatter/gather is weird when you put it into a list? The error is this about a dict being expected:

```
> cwltool mutationfinder3.cwl.yaml --mutation GCATCCA --normalin normal.fastq --tumorin tumor.fastq 
/anaconda/bin/cwltool 1.0.20151026181844
[job 4386765520] /var/folders/8r/g00jq11j2yb586cz04tfpydc0000gn/T/tmpm8fJ5E$ grep GCATCCA /Users/john/workspaces/commonwl_examples/t790m_detector/normal.fastq > /var/folders/8r/g00jq11j2yb586cz04tfpydc0000gn/T/tmpm8fJ5E/out.txt
[job 4386765840] /var/folders/8r/g00jq11j2yb586cz04tfpydc0000gn/T/tmpUclOsb$ grep GCATCCA /Users/john/workspaces/commonwl_examples/t790m_detector/tumor.fastq > /var/folders/8r/g00jq11j2yb586cz04tfpydc0000gn/T/tmpUclOsb/out.txt
Workflow error:
  Error validating input record, could not validate field `infile` because
  At position 0
    `[{'checksum': 'sha1$e62341a47da9cb155ec0f531b525a97d2bd86455',
      'class': 'File',
      'path': '/var/folders/8r/g00jq11j2yb586cz04tfpydc0000gn/T/tmpm8fJ5E/out.txt'[...]`
     is not a dict
```

Hello,

I have a doubt, I would like to get the vcf position of a cHGVS because I know they are the same regarding the effect in the transcript:
cHGVS="NM_000543.4:c.108_124del"
var_c1 = hp.parse_hgvs_variant(cHGVS)
cHGVS="NM_000543.4:c.126_143del"

issue_title: Do Duplications Exist in the BioCommons HGVS PAckage
issue_body: ## Does the HGVS Package recognize duplications? When there is an insertion that causes a duplication the hgvs_c should be annotated as such and should shift to the most 3' possible position as it isn't clear where the exact insertion is occurring. (see reference links [here](http://varnomen.hgvs.org/recommendations/DNA/variant/duplication/) and [here](http://varnomen.hgvs.org/recommendations/DNA/variant/insertion/) on this topic.) *Example:** HGVS Package Notation: NM_004448.2:2355_2356insCTGCTG Correct Notation: NM_004448.2:c.2358_2359dupCTGCTG (shifts to the end of the original CTTCTG sequence to be the most 3')

issue_title: `{start,stop}_sampling` has a different semantics in unix and windows
issue_body:
I just noticed by looking at the implementation: on unix, we use a counter, while on windows it's a simple boolean. This means that this code behave differently:

```python
_vmprof.stop_sampling()
_vmprof.stop_sampling()
_vmprof.start_sampling()
```

on widows, it will restart to take samples, while on unix it won't. I propose to tweak the windows implementation to behave like the unix one, to prevent subtle bugs and/or different behaviors.

issue_title: Marshmallow extensions does not support plural schema instances
sqlalchemy. Declarative support
issue_body:
according to http://flask.pocoo.org/docs/0.10/patterns/sqlalchemy/ , there are two ways documented to use SQLAlchemy in flask, one is with the flask-sqlalchemy extension, the other one is just Declarative, without flask-sqlalchemy. 

For me, I prefer the later one. Because it makes the Database layer much purer. 

In my case, I can extract an models.py file with [sqlacodegen](https://github.com/ksindi/sqlacodegen), and do the migrations with [alembic](https://alembic.readthedocs.io), without `flask-migrate`.

To do the database ops, the `flask` context is not always necessary. and with flask-sqlalchemy, I have to edit the auto-generated models file with all 'db.' prefix to fix the import problems. Well, it brings more cons than pros. 

But I still feel the `ModelSchema` and `HyperlinkModelSchema` are great code savier.

issue_title: How to optimize deep includes
issue_body:
Hi

We are using marshmallow-jsonapi with SQLAlchemy and we have few cases where we need to retrieve a groups of objects involving some deep includes. One such example is 

GET /v3/portfolios/672/investments?fields[accounts]=name&fields[files]=url&fields[indicators]=name&fields[investment_invitations]=organization&fields[reports]=name,stage,status&fields[results]=forecast_current&filter[allocations.results.indicator.relevant]=true&include=allocations.report,allocations.report.results,allocations.report.results.indicator,allocations.report.logo_file,invitation,grantee

The issues is that such calls takes ages.

I looked into `marshmallow-jsonapi` and understand how include data is gathered and how that can generate lot's of queries. 

I looked for ways to optimize that but did not see any obvious way to modify `fields.py`.

Any suggestion will be highly welcome.

issue_title: training_iterations in .mlmodel is always 1 when using GPU
issue_body:
Here's my scenario:

1. Using dataset from here: https://lear.inrialpes.fr/people/marszalek/data/ig02/
1. Following this: https://apple.github.io/turicreate/docs/userguide/object_detection/data-preparation.html for data preparation/generating SFrame
1. Following this: https://apple.github.io/turicreate/docs/userguide/object_detection/ to train the model

Training with turicreate 5.0b1 on a Late 2013 Macbook Pro with GT 750M. As expected, it is using GPU by default, doing 5000 iterations, and everything looks fine in the output, except that when used in a CoreML app, the model doesn't recognise anything.

Opening the .mlmodel file in an editor reveals that there's a line having `training_iterations1�`. At the same time, the .mlmodel file from [this example](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/vision/recognizing_objects_in_live_capture), has it as `training_iterations4000�` there, which made me suspect there's something wrong. I have then tried to re-run the training with `max_iterations` set to 15, but the output still had 1 (although `max_iterations` was 15 inside .mlmodel, as expected).

However, using `tc.config.set_num_gpus(0)` to force CPU-only computation yielded the correct result: both `training_iterations` and `max_iterations` was 15.

So it looks like a GPU rendering issue, but I am not sure if I there's any way to actually detect when and why it happens. On a side note, I am also experiencing [Issue #88](https://github.com/apple/turicreate/issues/88)

issue_title: Activity classifier should support test dataset with fewer unique labels than the training set
issue_body:
As described in #964 - the user has introduced larger number of labels in the test set than the number of labels the model have been trained on.  
Instead of giving him an explicit error right during the `predict()` portion of `evaluate()` - he got a less intuitive error:
```
ToolkitError: Size of prediction probability vector(3) != number of classes(1)
```

We should probably detect these cases explicitly, right in the beginning of `predict()`, and give an informative error.

issue_title: changePassword has inconsistent exceptions?
issue_body:
I'm using the `changePassword` function from this library and I came across an inconsistency in the exceptions it throws.

If you do this:

    try:
        result = kerberos.changePassword(
            uname, oldpw, newpw
        )
        return {"success": result}
    except kerberos.PwdChangeError as e:
        print(len(e.args))
        print(e.args)
        return {
            "error": str(e),
            "description": e.args[0],
            "value": e.args[-1] # my quick hack fix
        }

In every other case the exception will contain 2 arguments, except when the password change fails for policy reasons (complexity).

Example `print`s:

    2
    ('Preauthentication failed', -1765328360)
    1
    (('Password change rejected: ', 4),)

Apparently Microsoft's Active Directory KDC also doesn't provide a descriptive error text which should be included after the ": " so it's left blank. This puzzled me for a while also.

Tested against Active Directory KDC.

issue_title: Implement precompiled contracts
issue_body:
The EVM specification defines four so-called ‘precompiled’ contracts. The four contracts in addresses 1, 2, 3 and 4 execute the elliptic curve public key recovery function (ecrecover), the SHA2 256-bit hash scheme, the RIPEMD 160-bit hash scheme and the identity function respectively.

Call to the ecrecover precompiled contract in assembly:

CALL 0x1

Currently, LASER-Ethereum ignores calls to the precompiled contracts. In this task, the precompiled contracts are to be implemented. When a precompiled contract is called, the result should be computed using the appropriate Python cryptographic libraries. Also create tests for each of the four precompiles.

Go-Ethereum implementation:

https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/blob/master/core/vm/contracts.go

issue_title: Try using `connection.execute_wrapper`
issue_body:
Django 2.0 introduced [database instrumentation](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/2.0/topics/db/instrumentation/), a concept that could replace the monkey patching technique used in django-cachalot.

Instead of rewriting connection cursors via monkey patching, this would be a clean and officially supported technique that would also allow raw SQL caching.

Note that it will likely be impossible to completely replace monkey patching with this new technique, because that feature gives no access to the current `QuerySet`, `Query`, or transaction level.

But that's still worth exploring, and maybe we should improve database instrumentation so that future django-cachalot releases will no longer use monkey patching.

issue_title: Clarify module_parameters['EgRef'] usage, and set a default value
issue_body:
The CEC module parameters database does not contain the parameters EgRef or dEgdT. In the CEC model, and in the process NREL follows to generate the database from the CEC module data list, values EgRef = 1.121 and dEgdT = -0.0002677 are implicit.  PVLib for Matlab exposed EgRef and dEgdT as a model parameter (which it is) and let the user determine the appropriate value.  pvlib-python does likewise, however, readthedocs.io examples do not inform the user that s/he must specify EgRef and dEgdT nor do we provide default values when they are not specified.

I propose that we:
1) assign default values within `pvsystem.retrieve_sam('cecmod')` and
2) add to the example at [http://pvlib-python.readthedocs.io/en/latest/modelchain.html](url) to illustrate use of the CEC module model, and the opportunity to specify EgRef and dEgdT, if desired.

issue_title: consider setting lowest version for numpy and scipy
issue_body:
**current**: install requires list numpy but no version number
**proposed**: use `numpy==1.10` or even earlier, like `numpy==`1.8.2` or `numpy==1.9.5`

Not everyone is using conda, and the current versions of numpy on PyPI are built with ATLAS instead of MKL and more importantly, there is no corresponding scipy for the ATLAS build on Windows because there is no _open source_ MSVC compatible version of FORTRAN :(

Therefore, when you update pvlib using `pip install -U pvlib` into your Windows system  if there is a new version of numpy on PyPI then  it will remove your MKL version and install the ATLAS version, therefore breaking your scipy. This is annoying at best and confusing and possibly very problematic at worst, especially for novice python users.

Setting the minimum version required should resolve this for most people. Perhaps a section in the wiki or docs may also be useful?

issue_title: Confusing result when combining regular expressions and string results
issue_body:
Hi,

The following problem

```
(declare-const st (String))
(declare-const capture1 (String))
(declare-const capture2 (String))

(define-fun regexPortion1 () (RegEx String) (str.to.re "abcdefg"))
(define-fun in_portion_1 ((a String)) Bool (str.in.re a regexPortion1))

(define-fun regexPortion2 () (RegEx String) (re.++ regexPortion1 regexPortion1))
(define-fun in_portion_2 ((a String) (b String)) Bool (and (str.in.re a regexPortion2) (str.in.re b regexPortion1) (= a (str.++ b b))))

(define-fun regexPortion3 () (RegEx String) (re.* regexPortion2))
(define-fun in_portion_3 ((a String) (b String) (c String)) Bool (and (str.in.re a regexPortion3) (or (= a "") (and (in_portion_2 b c) (str.in.re a (re.+ (str.to.re b)))))))

cp ../src/api/ml/z3.mli api/ml/z3.mli
ocamlc  -I api/ml -o api/ml/z3.cmi -c api/ml/z3.mli
ocamlc  -I api/ml -o api/ml/z3.cmo -c ../src/api/ml/z3.ml
ocamlc  -ccopt "-D_MP_INTERNAL -D_AMD64_ -DNDEBUG -D_EXTERNAL_RELEASE -std=c++11 -fvisibility=hidden -c -mfpmath=sse -msse -msse2 -D_NO_OMP_ -O3 -D _EXTERNAL_RELEASE -fomit-frame-pointer -Wno-unknown-pragmas -Wno-overloaded-virtual -Wno-unused-value -fPIC -I /usr/local/lib/ocaml -I ../src/api -I ../src/api/ml -o api/ml/z3native_stubs.o" -c ../src/api/ml/z3native_stubs.c

Removing `-std=c++11` from `config.mk` fixed this problem, but it was non obvious until I asked on IRC.

issue_title: Unable to implement LSTM weight cloning/sharing
issue_body:
Hey, 

For training sequence to sequence RNNs it is very good to have batches as random as possible. So for a event based (only look at the output at a fixed time point) training I want to have single output for training and a continuous (sequence to sequence) output for testing my LSTM layer. It would therefore be necessary to share the weigths of two LSTM layers. I tried to implement this as similar as possible to  to the lasagne example and according to the concept of theano shared variables for the DenseLayer but it did not work out... Attached a plug and play code of what I'm trying to do. Thanks for any help and thanks for Lasagne! 

```
import lasagne
import numpy as np

def lstm_weight_sharing():

    grad_clipping = 0
    n_hidden = 8    # number of hidden units
    batch_size = 9
    sequence_len = 10
    n_feat = 11     # number of features

    # this is how I intuitively think it should be done stolen from the example code in lasagne
    l_in_dense = lasagne.layers.InputLayer((batch_size, n_feat))
    l_train = lasagne.layers.DenseLayer(l_in_dense, n_hidden)
    l_test = lasagne.layers.DenseLayer(l_in_dense, n_hidden, W=l_train.W, b=l_train.b)

    print [[param_train.get_value() == param_test.get_value()] for param_train, param_test in
           zip(lasagne.layers.get_all_params(l_train), lasagne.layers.get_all_params(l_test))]  #all True

    forget_gate = lasagne.layers.Gate()     # init the gates outside of LSTM to use them for train and test
    input_gate = lasagne.layers.Gate()      # init the gates outside of LSTM to use them for train and test
    output_gate = lasagne.layers.Gate()     # init the gates outside of LSTM to use them for train and test
    cell = lasagne.layers.Gate(W_cell=None, nonlinearity=lasagne.nonlinearities.tanh)

    # sequence input layer
    l_in = lasagne.layers.InputLayer((batch_size, sequence_len, n_feat))

    # sequ to label layer, only return final. This is for more representative, flexible training
    l_LSTM_train = lasagne.layers.LSTMLayer(l_in, n_hidden,
                                           nonlinearity=lasagne.nonlinearities.tanh,
                                           grad_clipping=grad_clipping,
                                           hid_init=lasagne.init.Constant(0.),  # to make sure the behave the same
                                           only_return_final=True,
                                           ingate=input_gate, outgate=output_gate, forgetgate=forget_gate,
                                           cell=cell)

    # copy of the layer above but sequ to sequ for testing
    l_LSTM_test = lasagne.layers.LSTMLayer(l_in, n_hidden,
                                           nonlinearity=lasagne.nonlinearities.tanh,
                                           grad_clipping=grad_clipping,
                                           hid_init=lasagne.init.Constant(0.),  # to make sure the behave the same
                                           only_return_final=False,
                                           ingate=input_gate, outgate=output_gate, forgetgate=forget_gate,
                                           cell=cell)

    l_LSTM_test.W_in_to_cell = l_LSTM_train.W_in_to_cell  # treat them like theano shared variables
    l_LSTM_test.b_cell = l_LSTM_train.b_cell  # treat them like theano shared variables
    l_LSTM_test.W_hid_to_cell = l_LSTM_train.W_hid_to_cell  # treat them like theano shared variables

    print 'some params of LSTM layer train'
    print [[param, param.get_value()[0]] for param in lasagne.layers.get_all_params(l_LSTM_train)]

    print 'some params of LSTM layer test'  # they should look like the ones of LSTM_train but don't...
    print [[param, param.get_value()[0]] for param in lasagne.layers.get_all_params(l_LSTM_test)]
```

Hi all!

I discovered pyfilesystem2 and it is great!
However I am experiencing some problems with FTP file systems.
In particular I am experiencing different behavior depending on the type if FTP server I am talking with.
I will report a case that is easy to reproduce.

Client: pyfilesystem2 (last committed version from github) on python 3.5.1 32bin Windows 7 
Server: pyftpdlib  (last committed version from github) on python 3.5.1 32bin Windows 7


```
from fs.copy import copy_fs

addr = '127.0.0.1'
usr = 'anonymous'
psw = "123"
ftproot = "/data/"

try:
    ftpurl = 'ftp://' + usr + ":" + psw + "@" + addr + ftproot
    print("copy files between ftp "+ftpurl+" an local filesystems... ", end='')
    
    copy_fs(ftpurl, 'osfs://d:/tmp2/target/')
    print("OK")
except Exception as ex:
    print("FAILS")  
```

Run ftp server as:

```
mkdir d:\\ftproot\\data

# one 50Kb jpg image
# 3 byte txt file
# 40MB txt file

python -m pyftpdlib  -p 21 -d d:\\ftproot

Could anybody suggest why?

Note that i have similar problems with a production proftpd server (very old version).

Thanks
Giampaolo

issue_title: run-athena-new does not pick up user code
issue_body:
Hi,

It turns out the way run-athena-new is coded up, it will not pick up the user's code in the worker. https://github.com/ganga-devs/ganga/blob/3b53affeff744a4cf80b83c94bd54142c567d37d/python/GangaAtlas/Lib/Athena/run-athena-new.sh#L35

This is the problematic part. 

1. If the user hasn't requested compilation, there should not be any build attempt
2. If the user has sent their workarea with the job, then all which needs to happen is the setup script should be sourced i.e. ```source usr/WorkDir/21.2.0/InstallArea/x86_64-slc6-gcc62-opt/setup.sh```

The problem though is that "path" probably will change depending on what release is set up, and I wouldnt want to definitively say how (especially for nightlies, I am not sure what it does properly). But basically you need to get through to the InstallArea and source the setup script then you are done.

Can this be looked into ASAP because at the moment it means ganga cannot be used with cmake releases :-(
Thanks
Will

issue_title: How do you want me to approach Yeelight bulbs?
issue_body:
Hi,

Yeelight bulbs listen on a different port for SSDP callouts (1982 vs 1900).

`M-SEARCH * HTTP/1.1
HOST: 239.255.255.250:1982
MAN: "ssdp:discover"
ST: wifi_bulb`

And they respond:

`HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Cache-Control: max-age=3600
Date:
Ext:
Location: yeelight://192.168.1.239:55443
Server: POSIX UPnP/1.0 YGLC/1
id: 0x000000000015243f
model: color
fw_ver: 18
support: get_prop set_default set_power toggle set_bright start_cf stop_cf set_scene
cron_add cron_get cron_del set_ct_abx set_rgb 
power: on
bright: 100
color_mode: 2
ct: 4000
rgb: 16711680
hue: 100
sat: 35
name: my_bulb`

So, while it *kind* of works like normal SSDP, it doesn't follow many standards.

Instead of adding a bunch of if code to support the bulbs in the ssdp.py module, I made a separate module. The thing is, most of the code comes from the ssdp module, just with minor changes.

If there was an interest in adding auto-discovery support for these bulbs, how would you want to approach it?

issue_title: 3rd reflection regression
issue_body:
As I mentioned [a couple weeks ago](https://github.com/sqlalchemy-redshift/sqlalchemy-redshift/pull/71#issuecomment-162223388), I got another regression, here's the minimal schema test case:

    create table foo.domain(
    id integer identity(1,1) not null unique);
    
    create table foo.bar(
    domain_id integer not null,
    foreign key(domain_id) references foo.domain(id))

(I almost got it working last time, but then life intervened and didn't touch this code since :(  )

This is quite weird, since `redshiftmeta.reflect(only=['domain'], schema='foo')` works just fine, but `redshiftmeta.reflect(only=['bar'], schema='foo')` fails with

    *** NoSuchTableError: foo."domain"

Also, the same with `foo.domain2` will work just fine... so I'd guess that there's some special-cased/hardcoded for stuff named `domain`, though by grepping briefly through the code that doesn't seem to be the case

I fixed it in a very dirty way:

```
import sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql.base as ps

REDSHIFT_RESERVED_WORDS = set("""aes128,aes256,all,allowoverwrite,analyse,
analyze,and,any,array,as,asc,authorization,backup,between,binary,blanksasnull,
both,bytedict,bzip2,case,cast,check,collate,column,constraint,create,

current_user_id,default,deferrable,deflate,defrag,delta,delta32k,desc,disable,
distinct,do,else,emptyasnull,enable,encode,encrypt,encryption,end,except,
explicit,false,for,foreign,freeze,from,full,globaldict256,globaldict64k,grant,
group,gzip,having,identity,ignore,ilike,in,initially,inner,intersect,into,is,
isnull,join,leading,left,like,limit,localtime,localtimestamp,lun,luns,lzo,lzop,
minus,mostly13,mostly32,mostly8,natural,new,not,notnull,null,nulls,off,offline,
offset,oid,old,on,only,open,or,order,outer,overlaps,parallel,partition,percent,
permissions,placing,primary,raw,readratio,recover,references,respect,rejectlog,
resort,restore,right,select,session_user,similar,some,sysdate,system,table,tag,
tdes,text255,text32k,then,timestamp,to,top,trailing,true,truncatecolumns,union,
unique,user,using,verbose,wallet,when,where,with,without""".split(","))

ps.PGIdentifierPreparer.reserved_words = ps.PGIdentifierPreparer.reserved_words.union(helpers.REDSHIFT_RESERVED_WORDS)

Would be good if the redshift dialect would support this out of the box.

Right now graphql-core in the lex pass completely omits comments. If you want to consume the content of comments in tooling, you are out of the luck.

Python graphql shoudl mimic graphql-js and do this. Lee Byron did this here https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/165b9d3a0b913d792501b0ce2829ad417d83122d by making the stream of lex tokens a doubly-linked list so you can "peek" backwards and inspect the contents of comments.

issue_title: Problem with creating CustomGraphQlView
issue_body:
Looks like it is impossible to create custom CBV inherited from GraphQLView 

_(Schema in example below is correct GraphQLSchema)_
**This example will not work**
```python 
# views.py
class CustomGraphQLView(GraphQLView):
    schema = Schema
    graphiql = True if settings.DEBUG else False

# urls.py
...CustomGraphQLView.as_view()...
```
For now the only way  to pass schema and graphiql attributes is using .as_view() method, that is definitely not how CBV should behave

**This one will work**
```python
# urls.py
...CustomGraphQLView.as_view(schema = Schema, graphiql = True if settings.DEBUG else False)...
```

After short investigation i problem was localized to this part of code:
https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene-django/blob/master/graphene_django/views.py#L67-L75

When using 1st example, in \_\__init\__\_ self.schema is defined (equals to Schema object) but local schema variable (attribute of \_\__init_\_\_ method) is None, so check on L69 is always is True and on L75  self.schema is being rewritten. In my case, graphene_settings.SCHEMA is not defined so self.schema after L75 is always None and check on [L84](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene-django/blob/master/graphene_django/views.py#L84) always raises AssertionError

There is the same problem with attributes like ```graphiql``` that are being overwritten by \_\__init_\_\_.

**Possible fix:**
```self.schema = self.schema or schema  # L75```
```self.graphiql = self.graphiql or graphiql  # L81```
etc



python 3.6
graphene-django==1.3

issue_title: graphene-django does not support multipart file upload per spec
issue_body:
A popular approach for handling file uploads when using Apollo Client is to utilize the [Apollo Upload Client Middleware](https://github.com/jaydenseric/apollo-upload-client) that will `POST` a multi-part form upload, along with the GraphQL query. In this scenario, the query lives in a different part of the `POST` body than as generally expected by graphene-django's `GraphQLView` class. 

The body includes notions of `operations` and a `map` that correlates data to each individual operation, as defined by the [multipart form upload spec for GraphQL](https://github.com/jaydenseric/graphql-multipart-request-spec).

It seems as though the problem here lies in the [`get_graphql_params` static method](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene-django/blob/master/graphene_django/views.py#L292), which is unable to extract GraphQL parameters from the request it received. I spent about a half hour seeing if I could sort this out, but wasn't able to square it away.

I would recommend adding functionality that detects when the body is consistent with this spec, and to add appropriate parsing capability.

At this point, my workaround is to skip using Apollo Client all together for file uploads, and to post a multi-part form upload query with a GraphQL body. This works, but it seems to be inconsistent with the spec, and it's not really as tightly integrated as I would like.

issue_title: DjangoFilterConnectionField fetches the entire model before slicing
issue_body:
https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene-django/blob/master/graphene_django/fields.py#L51

This condition is wrong for `DjangoFilterConnectionField`; `iterable` is a `FilterSet` descendant, which does not inherit from `QuerySet`. This causes a very expensive `len(iterable)` call, which `FilterSet` relays to the underlying queryset, which calls `self._fetch_all()` instead of `self.count()`. This all happens before slicing is applied, so asking for pagination does not improve performance.

Looks like the condition should be `isinstance(iterable, (QuerySet, FilterSet))`? Filter sets do have a `.count()` method, so they can follow the same interface as query sets.

issue_title: No edges appear in the graph explorer for Chrome 62.
issue_body:
![bnojao5eudk](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4221553/29738560-3c5699f4-89da-11e7-982e-0c40a784fdfe.png)

The problem is that Chrome 62 no longer supports the native `getPathSegAtLength` method. SVG 2 dropped the method. We should instead [make use of `getTotalLength()` and `getPointAtLength()` on the `SVGGeometryElement` object](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/SVGGeometryElement).

This issue must be fixed quickly, and then we should release a new pypi package as well as internal version. Otherwise, once Chrome 62 enters stable, no data flow edges will appear in the graph explorer for users (the graph would be very much broken). Chrome 62 has already entered the dev channel.

issue_title: graph zoom and resize resets after browser refresh
issue_body:
Hi, 

This is a usability problem related to #80. At the moment, when your friendly neighbor ML grad student refreshes tensorboard for the updated graph (because board refreshes too slowly even after --set-interval=1 as mentioned in #80 #158), the graph state gets lost. It then requires switching from keyboard to mouse and a few seconds of fumbling to get the graph zoom it as before.

The expected behavior is to have the board reload instantaneously, without losing the zoom and translation of the current graph view. Current behavior (loss of zoom and translation) incurs a large mental penalty to the user, it feels like getting zapped by the browser each time you refresh, like with the office doorknob in a dry and freezing Chicago winter.

It would be great if this state is somewhat preserved in the local storage or the url, so that we don't have to do this.

With stateless components, you could even have the entire client-side session state saved.

issue_title: Expose control over Django bulk_save option
issue_body:
I'm running into an issue with Django's QuerySet.bulk_create() on multi-table inheritance which [might be addressed in some cases](https://code.djangoproject.com/ticket/28821). The use of this feature is controlled using the bulk_save option on [pyexcel_io.database.importers.django.DjangoModelWriter](https://github.com/pyexcel/pyexcel-io/blob/a9bbbc4de7afcde289d745264d5bb115bbc0b8f1/pyexcel_io/database/importers/django.py#L20)...however, this is not exposed by pyexcel_io.database.importers.django.DjangoBookWriter:

>             sheet_writer = DjangoModelWriter(
>                self.__importer, model,
>                batch_size=self._keywords.get('batch_size', None))

A simple patch to address this would be to change that to:

>             sheet_writer = DjangoModelWriter(
>                self.__importer, model,
>                batch_size=self._keywords.get('batch_size', None),
>                bulk_save=self._keywords.get('bulk_save', None))

or (unless there is some reason to limit what is passed?) possibly even:

>             sheet_writer = DjangoModelWriter(
>                self.__importer, model, **self._keywords)

Higher up, the ability to specify bulk_save is present in pyexcel.internal.core.save_sheet and save_book but is not exposed by pyexcel.internal.meta.BookMeta.save_to_django_models. This would need to be trivially addressed too.

FWIW, I'm presently using pyexcel-io 0.5.4, but I see that master (as above) also currently has this issue.

issue_title: Git protocols
issue_body:
I have a few suggestions for good git protocols, especially as our project may be growing to include more contributors.  

1. Commits should have descriptive names that briefly explain what was done

2. Commit names like “Fixes #100” should have additional context so that they make sense when you aren’t looking at the git log through Github.

3. Commits should deal with one concern at a time — spanning multiple files is fine, but commits with many unrelated changes are confusing and should be broken up.  This makes it easier for someone else to understand what happened, and if something broke in a particular commit it is easier to track it down if the changes are more isolated.  

Anybody have others?  I think it will be helpful for us to try to be more strict on these things :)  I am happy to incorporate these items into CONTRIBUTING.

issue_title: Trading Off Precision and Recall
issue_body:
Hello, 

I trained a binary classifier and these are the metrics of the model:

Precision = TP (14263) / (TP (14263) + FP (1834)) = 0.886065
Recall = TP (14263) / (TP (14263) + FN (1880)) = 0.883540
F1 = (2 * 0.886065 * 0.883540) / (0.886065 + 0.883540) = 0.884800
Accuracy = TP (14263) + TN (9559)  / (TP (14263) + FN (1880) + TN (9559) + FP (1834) ) = 0.865122

I would like to have the higher recall and I'm ok for the precision to go down.

As I understand I can't control this trade-off via fastText supported parameters, but maybe I could change some of the hard coded values in model.cc file? Any ideas / hints are highly appreciated.

issue_title: Strange probabilities returned
issue_body:
I have noticed a strange issue in the way that fastText is handling probabilities and have seen this across all of the models that I have developed. There appears to be a cliff where sensible probabilities are returned and then after that the VAST majority have a 1.95313e-08 score.

Here is an example result:

 [0.371094, 0.0195313, 0.0136719, 0.0117188, 0.00976564, 0.00781252, 0.00781252, 0.00781252, 0.00585939, 0.00390627, 0.00390627, 0.00390627, 0.00390627, 0.00390627, 0.00390627, 0.00390627, 0.00195314, 0.00195314, 0.00195314, 0.00195314, 0.00195314, 0.00195314, 0.00195314, 0.00195314, 0.00195314, 0.00195314, 0.00195314, 0.00195314, 0.00195314, 0.00195314, 0.00195314, 1.95313e-08, 1.95313e-08, 1.95313e-08, ...]

The 1.95313e-08 score is there for about 20k classes.

I have seen this in every model that I have developed and the same value is returned even if the number of class labels are different (i.e. the sum across all of the scores does not = 1).

My questions are:

1. Why is it doing this?
2. Is there a way to get the actual confidence values across all of the class labels?

issue_title: Document RootLogger limitations
issue_body:
The logging.root is a module global RootLogger instance created at import time.

As such it's type is Logger, not talisker.Structured logger. This means that it doesnot support logfmt tags. 

But replacing it is hack, and fraught with corner cases, so we will leave it allone. An application should be using the root logger anyway.

But we should document this limitation.

issue_title: Ubuntu 14.04 fix
issue_body:
yycman December 27, 2015

Thanks for the tutorial. Very helpful.

Seems the default version of python supplied with Ubuntu 14.04 may need some extra updates to run letsencrypt.
I needed to update python's ssl libraries:

apt-get install python-dev libffi-dev libssl-dev
apt-get install python-pip
pip install 'requests[security]'
pip install pyopenssl ndg-httpsclient pyasn1

Then I needed to import ssl into python.

python
import ssl
quit()

Also, I found my domain name example.com must have an A record on the DNS server
and it must be the same IP address as the A record for www.example.com
A CNAME DNS record for www.example.com is not sufficient -- it must be an A record.

issue_title: Clearly document what APIs we consider public
issue_body:
For `acme` (and `josepy`), I'd say everything is public (besides objects prefixed with an underscore).

For the plugins, I'd say nothing is public besides the plugin API they implement.

For `certbot`, this is less clear. Everything in `achallenges.py`, `errors.py`, and `interfaces.py` should probably be considered public. Additionally, maybe some combination of `certbot/plugins/common.py`, `certbot/crypto_util.py`, and `certbot/util.py`?

We should clearly document this for people wanting to hack on/around the probject.

issue_title: certbot renew should tell you what to do when attempting to renew certs after an upgrade
issue_body:
I had installed the older version of certbot following the instructions for Debian 7 on... a debian 7 system. And I had generated some certificates which are working fine.
Then I upgraded to debian 8, so I deleted the old version of certbot, and I installed the new one via apt-get.

Now when I run `certbot renew`, I get this warning:
```
Attempting to parse the version 0.26.1 renewal configuration file found at /etc/letsencrypt/renewal/www.matteosistisette.com.conf with version 0.10.2 of Certbot. This might not work.
```
Of course it makes sense, but the error message should also tell me what I'm supposed to do to update the configuration file so that it's compatible with the new version and I stop getting this error.

issue_title: Document certbot's exit status codes
issue_body:
**Recommendation:** certbot's command line exit status codes should be added to the user documentation.

**Use case:** I have a shell script that does something when certbot updates the certificates that are about to expire. The easiest way I could think to do that would be to run the `cerbot renew` command, and check its exit status.

I observe that the exit code is 1 when the certbot did *not* need to update the certs. I assume the status would be 0 when it does update the certs. Users could possibly also add contingencies to their scripts and tools in case something went wrong and there were other status codes.

issue_title: SSLCipherSuite
issue_body:
**##The version of Certbot:** 
certbot 0.19.0
**## My operating system is:** 
Centos 7 - Kernel 3.10.0-229.11.1.el7.x86_64

Certbot installs a line to include the configuration of the file 

> options-ssl-apache.conf

According to https://www.ssllabs.com the cipher **DES-CBC3-SHA** is weak and should be disable but by default this file include it. Manual modification is not recommended and the include line is automatically added every time you new the certificates.

So, _I recommend to not include in future versions this cipher._

Thanks!

issue_title: [enhancement] Add a subdomain to an existing certificate
issue_body:
Hi,

first, thanks for this awesome tool that helps the web moving to the right direction.

As many other users, I am stuck because of the certificate limit rate. On my website example.com users can generate their own platform myplatform.example.com

To activate https on the fly, I have to create a new certificate, which makes me reach the limit in no time.

I'm using this command :
./letsencrypt-auto  certonly --agree-tos --email 'xxx@example.com' --webroot --webroot-path "/data/www-env/myplatform/public/" -d "myplatform.example.com" --debug

If I could add to an existing certificate the subdomain, it would be clearly a better option than this.
Any possibility to have this feature implemented? (Or a premium option for unlimited certificate issuance would also be a good solution).

Thanks for your amazing work,
Michael

issue_title: Make a troubleshooting script in the tools directory
issue_body:
Sometimes Certbot development virtual environments need to be recreated due to a change in our dependencies (which isn't always handled automatically due to pip's lack of dependency resolution) or entrypoints. When this happens, devs can get strange errors from `tox` or running `certbot` from their virtual environment that aren't at all clear what the problem is.

We should make a simple cleanup/troubleshooting shell script in `tools` that moves `.tox` and the existing `venv` directory, recreates the `venv`, and instructs the user to activate the new virtual environment. `tools/venv.sh` should help with a lot of this.

In addition, we should add documentation in our Developer Guide explaining that this occasionally happens and they can run this script to recreate their developer environment.

issue_title: Investigating type inference
issue_body:
This is mainly an investigation issue (for now).

pylint already does some typechecking, but it doesn't do true type inference; it can't detect an error in something like:

```python
def f(x):
    a = x + 3
    b = x[0]   # this can't work if x + 3 does
    return a + b
```

And yet it *does* have a type checker: https://github.com/PyCQA/pylint/blob/master/pylint/checkers/typecheck.py

Pick a particular checker in there (like `no-member`), and figure out how it works. I looked into it briefly, and it seems this will require understanding what the `infer` method does (astroid provides). Not too much documentation, so looking through the source code will be important.

Also play around with the code itself: you should be able to define your own checker based on the typechecker, and simplify and modify the code to see how it works.

Apparently, Schoology is mandating it as [part of the oauth signature](https://developers.schoology.com/api-documentation/authentication#toc-item-2). issue_title: Update shield images for python-tap
ConnectionManager should check for address being valid before connecting
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

The connection manager just choses addresses from the channelgraph. If the channelgraph contains invalid entries, we might pick these up.

## Solution

Check for any chosen address to be registered in discovery (plus optional healthcheck) before conecting.

issue_title: Make Compatible with Macos
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Reports from users are that Raiden at the moment is [not compatible with Macos](https://twitter.com/homakov/status/908304985892499456). A user in [gitter](https://gitter.im/raiden-network/raiden?at=59b7a9b2bc464729742303a6) reported that there is a dependency problem: 

`pkg_resources.DistributionNotFound: The 'scrypt' distribution was not found and is required by ethereum, pyethapp`

It's quite possible that there are other problems as we have not really tested on Macos. Since it's a popular distro among some devs we should make this a priority.

## Solution

Test raiden on Macos and make sure all functionality works there in the same way as it does in Linux.

## Tasklist

- [ ] Test Raiden in Macosx and fix all incompatibilities
- [ ] Run tests in Travis, or some other CI also for Macos. (Optional - perhaps make another issue for this)
- [ ] Add a changelog entry.

issue_title: Need for option -a (--all) for sos status/kill/purge?
issue_body:
Right now

```
sos status
```
lists all tasks with an justification that empty string matches all tasks.

This is however dangerous for `sos kill` so an option `--all` is added to `sos kill`, namely

```
sos kill
```

does not kill any task and 

```
sos kill --all
```
would kill all.

Not sure how 
```
sos purge
```
should behavior, because

```
sos purge --all
```
is safer but feels redundant even conflicting with other options such as 

```
sos purge --all --age +5d
```

issue_title: Inconsistent behavior of `stop_if`
issue_body:
Here is a MWE:

```
parameter: K1 = 1
parameter: K2 = 1
stop_if(K1, msg = 'stop here at global')

[1]
stop_if(K2, msg = 'stop here local')
```

Here is the outcome:

```
$ sos run test.sos --K1 1
ERROR: stop here at global
$ sos run test.sos --K1 0 --K2 1
INFO: Executing default_1: 
INFO: input:    []
WARNING: stop here local
INFO: output:   []
INFO: Workflow default (ID=9299f73c394453e4) is executed successfully.
```
It looks like when outside a workflow step `stop_if` can properly stop the workflow with an `ERROR` prompt. Otherwise it is just a `WARNING`, and although it does stop, the finally message would say `executed successfully`. Shouldnt the behavior consistent?

If there is an error in line 239 of dataset.py:
`im = cv2.imread(str(fn), flags).astype(np.float32)/255`
it cannot be handled by the next line  
`if im is None: raise OSError(f'File not recognized by opencv: {fn}')`

im = cv2.imread(str(fn), flags)

else:

issue_title: social_django and Gmail API
issue_body:
I'm building a tool to look into email data kind of like Unroll.me if you are familiar with it.

To start out my team and I began using the django-social-auth library and found that it could authenticate us into an account and stored the account meta data in the Django projects database.  

Next we found the Google Gmail API had some sample code here: https://developers.google.com/gmail/api/quickstart/python 

This code worked to allow access to our users Gmail data but didn't store the email or meta data in the database to authenticate the user within the Django application. 

These two approaches apparently use both the Google Plus API and the Gmail API.  Each of these has a different URL.  Is there a way to authenticate against one API and store the credential to authenticate against another?

Is there a way to marry the two so users only need to sign in once but Django can store the email/meta data in the database and we can go through emails stored in Gmail?

Thanks for any help you can offer!

issue_title: Pluggable config/secret storage
issue_body:
We have a reasonable default mechanism for config/secret storage today, where we use KMS for encryption, and just tick them in the resulting config YAML file.  Although simple, we will want a lot more flexibility in the future.  For instance, we may want to support secrets stored in Hashicorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, and so on.  I assume this will need to be a true point of extensibility.

This goes hand in hand with https://github.com/pulumi/pulumi/issues/397, which is to defer decryption until "as late as possible," which touches the pluggable interface here in the sense that I assume we want all access to secrets managed by, say, Vault, to go through Vault at the time of usage, to integrate properly with its auditing capabilities.  This is a service pulumi.com should also provide, once we have the ability to properly defer this to runtime rather than deployment time.

issue_title: Should we provide functionality for running a function on all workers (or all nodes)?
issue_body:
Should we expose the ability to run a given function on all workers? This can be useful for a bunch of reasons (for example, properly setting the Python path on each worker).

Should we also expose the ability to run a given function once on each node? This can be useful for setting things up (e.g., installing some stuff, downloading some files, copying application files).

Of course, we shouldn't add any functionality without a very good reason...

issue_title: Crispy filter does not include form media after 1.6.0 release
issue_body:
The crispy filter does not include a form's media anymore since release 1.6.0. This appears to be a bug as in the previous release, 1.5.2, the crispy filter when applied to a form or formset's context variable would include the form's media when rendered. **In the template** ``` {% load crispy_forms_filters %} ... {{ form|crispy }} ``` **Form class** ``` class MyForm(forms.Form): date=forms.DateField( label='Approved', required=True, widget=DatePlaceholder, ) ... ``` The date field uses a widget that requires some javaScript and CSS to work correctly. These files are included in the form's media when constructed in the view. In version 1.5.2, this media would be included in the rendered html as seen below: ```
``` However, when using version 1.6.0, without changing any code, this is the rendered html: ```
``` As you can see, the form media is no longer being included when being rendered by the filter. This seems like it is a bug introduced between release 1.5.2 and 1.6.0. It initially seems like this could be related to the changes from #525 with the optional inclusion of form media when using the `crispy` template tag. Perhaps the include_media context variable is not being set correctly.

issue_title: SRP doesn't authenticate reliably with client secret
issue_body:
You current implementation of `process_challenge` doesn't reliably authenticate when using a `client_secret`. It will yield a:

"An error occurred (NotAuthorizedException) when calling the RespondToAuthChallenge operation: Unable to verify secret hash for client"

As you can see in the official AWS java SDK, the right way to respond to an auth challenge request (https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-android/blob/master/aws-android-sdk-cognitoidentityprovider/src/main/java/com/amazonaws/mobileconnectors/cognitoidentityprovider/CognitoUser.java#L2914 ) is to use the internal user name from the challenge paramteters and use it for secret hash calculation (https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-android/blob/master/aws-android-sdk-cognitoidentityprovider/src/main/java/com/amazonaws/mobileconnectors/cognitoidentityprovider/CognitoUser.java#L2955 ) as well as username in the `srpAuthResponse` (https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-android/blob/master/aws-android-sdk-cognitoidentityprovider/src/main/java/com/amazonaws/mobileconnectors/cognitoidentityprovider/CognitoUser.java#L2964 )

Please find attached the pull request that fixes this issue. https://github.com/capless/warrant/pull/124

issue_title: Conversion to xml missing text information
Extremely slow on large select, http protocol almost 10 times faster
issue_body:
It seems that selecting large datasets using the native client is extremely slow. Here is my benchmark https://gist.github.com/dmitriyshashkin/6a4849bdcf882ba340cdfbc1990da401

Initially, I've encountered this behavior on my own dataset, but I was able to reproduce it using the dataset and the structure described here https://clickhouse.yandex/docs/en/getting_started/example_datasets/ontime/

To simplify things a little bit I've used the data for just one month:  http://transtats.bts.gov/PREZIP/On_Time_On_Time_Performance_2017_12.zip

As you can see the fastest way to get the data is by using HTTP protocol with requests and pandas. The problem gets worse as the number of the rows grows, on my own dataset with 5M rows I waited for 1 hour before I had to interrupt the process. The bottleneck is not CH itself, the "top" command shows that all the work is done by python with 100% CPU utilization, while CH is almost idle.

issue_title: Add docstrings to everything
issue_body:
You can do:

`pip install pydocstyle`
`python pydocstyle.py` (in the pyt repo)
and it'll spit out which functions don't have docstrings or complain about something non-PEP 257 compliant.

This is a great way to learn the codebase, and everyone will love you for it.

The [imports code for example](https://github.com/python-security/pyt/blob/master/pyt/stmt_visitor.py#L716-L1013) does not have docstrings, for example.

For an example of great docstrings, see the [user-defined function calls code](https://github.com/python-security/pyt/blob/master/pyt/expr_visitor.py#L187-L495).

issue_title: Creating domwidgets on the frontend causes errors on the backend setting layout to null
issue_body:
Lets's start how I found out:
With [ipywebrtc](https://github.com/maartenbreddels/ipywebrtc) I do the following:
```python
import ipywebrtc
import ipywidgets as widgets
from IPython.display import display
camera = ipywebrtc.CameraStream()
room = ipywebrtc.WebRTCRoomMqtt(stream=camera, room='bug')
room2 = ipywebrtc.WebRTCRoomMqtt(stream=camera, room='bug')
#to see what happens uncomment below:
#box = widgets.HBox(children=[])
#widgets.jslink((room, 'streams'), (box, 'children'))
#display(box)
```
What will happen, is that room.peers and room.streams will be set at the frontend when people join (here two, yourself twice).
However, streams are [MediaStream](https://github.com/maartenbreddels/ipywebrtc/blob/88857f401c8669f9a771b14e9f58288dc5238e1d/ipywebrtc/webrtc.py#L17) instances, and are a subclass of DOMWidget. DOMWidget has a layout property. After the comm gets created, it will update the values with `layout: null`, causing an error:
`"The 'layout' trait of a MediaStream instance must be a Layout, but a value of class 'NoneType' (i.e. None) was specified."`

You'd think this would happen with the Controller as well, except what Controller creates are not DOMWidgets, so it has no layout property on the backend, although the frontend [says it is a coredomwidget](https://github.com/jupyter-widgets/ipywidgets/blob/8aaae57fb625302eb614a3aaddd299e088de2468/packages/controls/src/widget_controller.ts#L17) (bug?).

issue_title: Apply attribute to input transform uniformly / outline best practices
issue_body:
In https://github.com/onnx/onnx/pull/63 we came to the conclusion that, whenever possible, we should prefer representing operators as taking a zero-dim tensor input (i.e., scalar) rather than an attribute. The motivation is that scalars as tensor inputs is strictly more expressive than attributes, because their values can vary at runtime (as opposed to attributes, which are fixed), so you're going to need the tensor input variants anyway, and so you might as well drop the attribute.

However, the PR only handles Pow and Slice, but there are a number of other operators (e.g., `AddConstant`) which take scalars, and need to be harmonized by removing the `AddConstant` version and deferring it to `Add`. We may also need to determine a coherent story for broadcasting, because the most parsimonious way to give meaning to addition of a tensor with a zero-dim tensor is through broadcasting, but may operators today (e.g., Gemm) explicitly specify whether or not broadcasting on a particular argument takes place. (Though, we may be able to sidestep this issue by special casing scalar.)

Furthermore, writing ONNX in this style needs some logic from frontends-backends, which we should document, to help frontend/backend writers.

1. If you are a frontend, and you convert from an attribute as opposed to an input, you have to allocate a Scalar to pass into the operator. We should make sure it's clear and easy how to allocate a zero-dim tensor in this case.
2. If you are a backend, and you convert to a scalar to an attribute as opposed to an input, because, perhaps, you don't support broadcasting, or you have a more efficient non-broadcasting implementation. In this case, you need to implement a simple constant propagation analysis on ONNX, so that at any point in time, you know if tensor inputs are constants or not. This is perhaps best prototyped in onnx-caffe2.

https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/docs/Operators.md#BatchNormalization

> This version of the operator has been available since version 6 of the default ONNX operator set.
Other versions of this operator: BatchNormalization-1

our code is https://github.com/onnx/onnxmltools/blob/master/onnxmltools/convert/coreml/operator_converters/neural_network/BatchNorm.py#L69:

issue_title: [Layer Issue] Image Resampling - DownSampling2dLayer is KO
issue_body:
The layer `tl.layers.DownSampling2dLayer` is KO.

```python
import tensorflow as tf
import tensorlayer as tl

tf.logging.set_verbosity(tf.logging.DEBUG)
tl.logging.set_verbosity(tl.logging.DEBUG)

plh = tf.placeholder(tf.float16, [32, 16, 16, 3])

net_in = tl.layers.InputLayer(plh)
print("INPUT SHAPE: %s" % str(net_in.outputs.shape))

net_up = tl.layers.UpSampling2dLayer(net_in, size=(2, 2), is_scale=True, method=0, align_corners=True)
print("UPSAMPLING OUT SHAPE: %s" % str(net_up.outputs.shape))

try:
    net_down = tl.layers.DownSampling2dLayer(net_up, size=(0.5, 0.5), is_scale=True, method=0, align_corners=True)
    print("DOWNSAMPLING OUT SHAPE (float scale): %s" % str(net_down.outputs.shape))

except:
    net_down = tl.layers.DownSampling2dLayer(net_up, size=(2, 2), is_scale=True, method=0, align_corners=True)
    print("DOWNSAMPLING OUT SHAPE (int scale): %s" % str(net_down.outputs.shape))
```

Output:

```python
[TL] InputLayer  input: (32, 16, 16, 3)
INPUT SHAPE: (32, 16, 16, 3)
[TL] UpSampling2dLayer upsample2d_layer: is_scale: True size: (2, 2) method: 0 align_corners: True
UPSAMPLING OUT SHAPE: (32, 32, 32, 3)
[TL] DownSampling2dLayer downsample2d_layer: is_scale: True size: (0.5, 0.5) method: 0, align_corners: True
[TL] DownSampling2dLayer downsample2d_layer: is_scale: True size: (2, 2) method: 0, align_corners: True
DOWNSAMPLING OUT SHAPE (int scale): (32, 64, 64, 3)
```

Impossible to downscale with a float scale factor < 1 or an int scale factor > 1.
Basically `tl.layers.DownSampling2dLayer` and `tl.layers.UpSampling2dLayer` are identical.

**I have fix this issue in PR #755, however this not merged yet.**

issue_title: Mandatory check for redirect_uri happening while authorizing request
issue_body:
I am using Flask-OAuthlib which uses the `oauthlib`, after granting permissions it tries to check if `redirect_uri` is present in `request` while trying to fetch the auth token. The `redirect_uri` should be optional according to RFC - https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-4.1.1. 

This is where it breaks - 

```# REQUIRED, if the "redirect_uri" parameter was included in the
      # authorization request as described in Section 4.1.1, and their
      # values MUST be identical.
      if not self.request_validator.confirm_redirect_uri(request.client_id, request.code,
                                                         request.redirect_uri, request.client):
          log.debug('Redirect_uri (%r) invalid for client %r (%r).',
                    request.redirect_uri, request.client_id, request.client)
          raise errors.AccessDeniedError(request=request)```

issue_title: Inconsistency between Tensorflow and Theano/CNTK when using NASNetLarge/NASNetMobile and MobileNetV2 in Keras 2.2.2
issue_body:
I am using the NASNetLarge/NASNetMobile and the MobileNetV2 on Keras 2.2.2. I am testing with all three backends and I see the differences between TensorFlow and Theano/CNTK.

I reported that [here](https://github.com/keras-team/keras/issues/10672)

At first, I thought it was because of the Depthwise and Separately Conv2D that was implemented for CNTK and Theano in their backend files. But later I found out that it seems to #be because of padding SAME inconsistency between TensorFlow and the other two backends.

I have seen fixes in the Mobile and ResNet50 that simply add ZeroPadding and use padding VALID. [#9473](https://github.com/keras-team/keras/pull/9473)?

Can someone confirm that we can do the same for these networks?  Or should we actually implement the padding scheme SAME of TensorFlow on Theano and CNTK to be consistent?

issue_title: pass `order` to affine_transform
issue_body:
The method `apply_affine_transform` is a wrapper `scipy.ndimage.interpolation.affine_transform` that has a parameter `order` which is the order a a spline interpolation. Not being able to pass this parameter causes a problem when generating random transformation of labeled images since it results in non-integer values.

for example: 

for a given labels (each integer is a class label) 
`[[2 2 0 2 2]
 [1 3 2 3 1]
 [2 1 0 1 2]
 [3 1 0 2 0]
 [3 1 3 2 1]]`

it transformed to 
`[[2.289865   1.7110896  1.8507836  2.172145   0.15832195]
 [3.         2.2037435  1.0774351  1.4194988  2.3764393 ]
 [3.         1.4194988  0.39426237 0.49894607 1.7452569 ]
 [1.8929222  1.7380519  1.7849773  2.         1.2062019 ]
 [1.2646285  2.8956027  2.2367117  1.3144331  0.23671168]]`

Which are clearly not valid class labels. I dag around and found that the reason is the hard coded `order` in https://github.com/keras-team/keras-preprocessing/blob/master/keras_preprocessing/image.py line 323. 

being able to pass this parameter would enable correct data augmentation for image segmentation.

issue_title: default -march=native build cannot be overridden by flags
issue_body:
I would like to use nmslib in python and when installing nmslib by `pip install nmslib`, `-march=native` is passed to gcc and so binaries are created that work only on the same architecture. I would like to use nmslib inside docker and these docker containers would run on different types of machines (different generations of aws ec2 instances), but with this building setting will cause `Illegal instruction` in some cases.

It would be great if C++ build flags could be overridden somehow. I tried with `CFLAGS="-march=sse4.2"`, but these options get _before_ `-march=native"`, so the latter overrides the former.

I created my own fork of nmslib, modified `setup.py` and this solved the problem, and this clearly isn't the best solution, and maybe others have the same problem when using nmslib with docker and ec2, so I hoped someone here can suggest a better solution.

Thanks

issue_title: html entities being stripped when parsing rdfa with DOCTYPE
issue_body:
ed.summers, 2011-04-11T16:23:55.000Z 
I am not 100% certain if this is the accepted behavior here, but I've noticed that rdflib's RDFa parser is stripping HTML entities when processing XHTML, but that similar processing without the RDFa DOCTYPE results in the entity being translated to UTF-8.

See the attached test: entities.py ...

Comment 1 by ed.summers



Comment 2 by ed.summers



Comment 3 by ed.summers
After some poking around it looks like HTML with a DOCTYPE is successfully parsed by minidom (but the entity is stripped out). But when the HTML lacks a DOCTYPE it fails mindom parsing with a: 

    xml.parsers.expat.ExpatError: undefined entity: line 4, column 60

and is then parsed by html5lib (where the entity is successfully converted to utf8). So that explains why the entities are stripped out from RDFa HTML with the DOCTYPE and converted to UTF8 when there is no DOCTYPE.

Now to figure out what the fix should be, if any...


Comment 4 by ed.summers
Wondering if maybe we just hand off all html parsing to html5lib and make it an rdflib dependency?


Comment 5 by ed.summers
Looks like xml.minidom has no support for entities:

    The following interfaces have no implementation in xml.dom.minidom...Entity
    http://docs.python.org/library/xml.dom.minidom.html
